# Debitam - Online Account Filing
Online company accounts and tax return filing service for small businesses.
Accounting • 42-44 Bishopsgate, London • 3,382 followers • 51-200 employees

## Overview
Online Accounting Service | Bookkeeping | Payroll | Personal Tax Return Filing to HMRC | Corporation Tax Filing | HMRC ONLINE FILING: Our aim is to help small business owners` tax return and account filing compliance works by using cutting-edge technology and adopting a versatile corporate culture. WHO ARE WE: We are a web-based online filing service on which you can find real people, who are qualified accountants, behind it. WHY US: Online Account Filing distinct from its peers with its exclusively assigned accountant system to each client and its significantly lower prices. As much as being a digital platform, we also offer a wide range of tax advice during your filing journey with us. REVIEWS: Online Account Filing is the top accountancy firm on Trust Pilot for 5 months in a row with over 400 Excellent rated reviews and 4.9 Trust Score. LOCATION: Our HQ is one of the well-connected locations in Bishopsgate, London. SERVICES: - Company Accounts & Tax Return - Self-Assessment Tax Return - VAT & Bookkeeping services, by adjusting ourselves with your previous accounting application. - CIS compliance works - Payroll running - Confirmation Statement - Assisting and reminding your businesses tax return and company filing deadlines to help you to avoid missing any deadline with your businesses tax returns. KNOWLEDGE: Visit our Knowledge section to update yourself with the most recent developments in Taxation, Business Loans, Tax Claims and Small Business Tips: >>> https://www.onlineaccountfiling.com/blog/

## Posts
**Debitam - Online Account Filing** (1d): Most UK limited companies see bookkeeping as a compliance task. That’s a mistake. Bookkeeping isn’t just about filing on time — it’s about control, clarity, and growth. If your numbers are accurate and up to date, you can: ✔ Control cash flow with confidence ✔ Avoid costly VAT errors ✔ Understand true profitability ✔ Plan tax efficiently — not reactively Accurate books → Better decisions → Real business growth. At DEBITAM, we support UK limited companies with reliable, software-driven bookkeeping using Xero, QuickBooks, Sage and more — so you’re never guessing where your business stands. If your bookkeeping isn’t helping you grow, it’s time to fix that. Let’s connect. [Likes: 4, Comments: 0]; **Debitam - Online Account Filing** (3d 3d): 🚨 MTD IS NO LONGER “COMING SOON” — IT’S HAPPENING. And yes — you can still have the company pay for a client lunch, but it won’t reduce your tax bill. 👉 Get the full breakdown and examples in the blog: https://lnkd.in/e6bV6sK6 #UKTax #BusinessExpenses #SME #Accounting #TaxTips [Likes: 5, Comments: 0]; **Debitam - Online Account Filing** (3w Edited): 🚨A spike in calls from people thinking we’re HMRC — here’s why 🔊Today we’ve received an unusually high number of calls from individuals who believe they’re speaking to HM Revenue & Customs. To clarify: 🙅🏻♂️We are not HMRC. ✍️We are an independent accounting firm that supports businesses and individuals with tax filings and compliance with HMRC. 🦹🏼♂️This surge is almost certainly linked to the ongoing rise in HMRC impersonation scams, which typically increase around tax deadlines. Fraudsters often use phone number “spoofing” or alarming messages to pressure people into calling back — which can sometimes lead them to legitimate accounting firms like ours by mistake. A few important reminders for everyone: 🔺HMRC will never threaten arrest or demand immediate payment over the phone 🔺They usually contact you by letter first, not via unexpected calls or texts If you’re unsure, always use contact details listed directly on gov.uk 🔺We completely understand how stressful unexpected tax-related calls can be, and we’re happy to point callers in the right direction when needed. But please be aware: anyone claiming to be HMRC and demanding urgent action is very likely a scam. If this post helps even one person avoid fraud, it’s worth sharing. # How can we help?

Find advice and answers from our support team fast or get in touch

*   [General](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#General)
*   [Self Assessment](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#Self_Assessment)
*   [Corporation Tax](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#Corporation_Tax)
*   [IR35](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#IR35)
*   [VAT](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#VAT)

*   [Export-Import](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#Export_Import)
*   [Compliance](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#Compliance)
*   [Company](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#Company)
*   [CIS](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#CIS)
*   [COVID-19](https://www.debitam.com/faq/#COVID-19)

*    General 
*   What is UTR (Unique Taxpayer Reference)?  A UTR (Unique Taxpayer Reference) number is a 10 digit code that's unique to either you or your company. It facilitates HMRC to process tax returns in an efficient, effective and accurate manner. It helps to identify the person (i.e. whether it's a company, partnership etc.) so that their records can be managed accordingly. It also helps in ensuring that everyone pays what they owe or receive amounts they might be entitled to correctly. 
*   Why does the HMRC need my UTR (Unique Taxpayer Reference)? UTR number is a 10 digit unique code issued by HMRC to all the entities whether individuals, limited companies, LLPs etc. HMRC needs the UTR number to identify the person or sole proprietor for return filing and tax payment purposes. 
*   How do I know my tax-free income through tax codes?  

    *   A tax code is an alphanumeric code used to work out how much income tax needs to be collected from your income. 
    *   These codes generally start with a number and end with an alphabet, for e.g., 1257L. The number in a tax code denotes the amount of tax-free income you get in that particular year.
    *   The letter in the tax code refers to your personal circumstances which may affect your Personal Allowance.

*   What are different types of errors that can occur while finalising a Trial balance?  Various types of trial balance errors are as follows:

**The error of Principle:**

 When a transaction is recorded in a manner that any of the generally accepted accounting principles is violated then it is called error of principle. There isn't any effect on trial balance in such cases as the amount is correctly posted on debit-credit sides but the accounts are incorrect. Such errors can cause the financial statements to depict a misleading picture of the affairs of the business.

**Omission Error:**

 There can be two kinds of error of omission- complete and partial. Complete omission means that a transaction has been missed to be recorded in the journal. There isn't any effect on trial balance in such cases. Partial omission means that a transaction has been recorded in the journal but omitted to be posted into the ledger accounts. Such kinds of omissions hamper the agreement of trial balance.

**Commission error:**

 Such errors generally relate to arithmetic accuracy. These include recording wrong amount in subsidiary books, wrong totalling of subsidiary books, posting incorrect amount in ledger accounts, posting at the wrong side of ledger accounts, incorrect totalling of ledger balances etc. These may or may not cause trial balance totally.

**Compensating errors:**

 If two or more errors are committed in a manner that the effect of one error is compensated by the effect of other error, then such errors are called compensating errors. These errors leave agreement of trial balance unaffected. 
*   What are the effects of over-reporting and under-reporting in accounts submitted to authorities?  **Over reporting-** Once you file your accounts to Companies House and HMRC, they come into the public domain. Over reporting in the accounts can cause your competitors to know your business strategy and they could easily take undue advantage of this.

**Underreporting-** Underreporting to Companies House and HMRC can cause them to take action against you. You might end up paying heavy penalties. 
*   How will early filing of accounts benefit me? Or why should I file my accounts early?  

Filing your accounts early before the due date has its own benefits.

Some of them are:

    *   No worrying about deadlines.
    *   No fear of paying penalties for late filing. 
    *   Extra time to make any amendments. 
    *   **Cash Flow Management** - Nothing is ever guaranteed in business or life, apart from death and taxes. You will have to pay taxes on your profits that are a certainty. It’s advantageous to have all of the information as early as possible so that you can budget appropriately and ensure that you have the necessary funds ready to pay your tax bill when the time comes. 
    *   **Peace of mind** - Businesses can be stressful at times and you rarely know what is coming up in the future. This is an opportunity to gain more clarity over the financial future of your business and at least understand and factor future outgoing payments into your plans. 

*   What is the difference between the accrual basis of accounting and cash basis of accounting?  Cash basis of accounting deals with recognising revenues- when cash is actually received and expenses- when cash is actually paid. In this method, there is no requirement of keeping a track of receivables and payables. The business income isn't taxed until it's received in cash or in a bank account. It is easier to maintain. The disadvantage of this method is that it ends up giving a biased view of the business as an expense and its related revenue might get reported in different periods.

Accrual basis of accounting deals with recognising revenues when they are earned and expenses when they are incurred, irrespective of the date of actual payment. irrespective of the fact that whether the money has been received/paid. This method works on matching principle which means the expense is reported in the period when related revenues are earned. This method gives a true and significantly better view as compared to the cash basis of accounting. 
*   What is HMRC gateway account?  The HMRC Gateway account is an account created to use online services provided by HMRC. To [sign in the HMRC gateway](https://www.gov.uk/log-in-register-hmrc-online-services) one needs 12 digit Government Gateway user ID and password. 
*   Where can I find my UTR (Unique Taxpayer Reference) number?  

[UTR (Unique Taxpayer Reference)](https://www.debitam.com/faq/) is automatically sent when you [register for self-assessment](https://www.debitam.com/faq/) or [set up a limited company](https://www.debitam.com/article/formation-of-a-company/). It can be found on your:

    *   Previous returns 
    *   Payment reminders or notices to file returns received from HMRC at your registered office address.
    *   You can call the self-assessment helpline on 0300 200 3310 to request your UTR if you cannot find any documents from HMRC. 
    *   For a limited company, we can request your Corporation Tax UTR online. HMRC will send it to your business address that is registered with the Companies House.

*   What is a benefit in kind?  

Benefit in kind refers to:

    *   Asset or service provided by the employer to employee,
    *   that is used by employee personally but is paid for by a company
    *   and isn't “wholly, exclusively and necessary” for business purpose.

Benefits in kind need to be reported in the [form P11D](https://www.debitam.com/faq/). They are treated as cash equivalents and so are taxed as a part of your (employee’s) salary.

*   Does the employer need to report the HMRC for a trivial benefit?  

If the employer provides [trivial benefit](https://www.gov.uk/expenses-and-benefits-trivial-benefits) as a part of the salary sacrifice arrangement, then they won’t be exempt. The employer will have to report it to the HMRC in form P11D.

    *   The salary is given up or 
    *   How much you paid for the trivial benefits, whichever is higher. 

However, the director of a ‘close company’ cannot receive trivial benefits worth more than £300 in a tax year.

*   What is the minimum wage?  The government has prescribed minimum wage rates i.e., the minimum amount per hour that any worker should be paid. Workers above the age of 25 are entitled to National Living Wage which is highest of the National Minimum Wage. The wages amount determined using these minimum wage rates is called minimum wage. 
*   What are benchmark scale rates?  Benchmark Scale rate payments are basically the maximum tax and [NICs](https://www.debitam.com/article/national-insurance-contributions/) free amount that can be paid or reimbursed by the employers to their employees in respect of common business expenses incurred by the employee, without requiring any approval from HMRC. 
*   What is Auto-enrolment of pensions?  

As per the Pension regulators guidelines, the employer has to auto-enrol all its employees under a workplace pension scheme and also contribute a prescribed percentage of the salary as an employer's contribution. Any employers in the UK fulfilling the below conditions are bound by this legislation:

    1.   if they employ at least 1 person
    2.   such person/persons earn more than £10,000 a year
    3.   such person/persons are aged between 22 and State Pension Age

*   How do I know which period is considered as the financial year? The period falling between two [accounting reference dates](https://www.debitam.com/faq/) is considered as the financial year. It is also known as an accounting period. 
*   How do I know which period is considered as an accounting year?  The period falling between two [accounting reference dates](https://www.debitam.com/faq/) is considered as an accounting year. It is also known as a financial year. 
*   Can I claim for deductions as Capital allowance?  Capital allowance refers to deductions allowed from the profits with respect to certain assets purchased which have a useful life of more than one tax period. [Click here](https://www.debitam.com/article/capital-allowance/) to read about capital allowances in detail. 
*   How do I know which day is considered as the accounting reference date?  When your company is formed, the first accounting reference date will be a year later, on the last day of the month in which your company was incorporated. The accounting reference date will then be on this date every year until we change the accounting period. 
*   What do you mean by Business mileage claim/allowance?  Business mileage is basically a blanket term for the expenses reimbursed by an employer for travel expenses of the employee for purpose of business travel. For sole traders, it shall cover the cost of buying and maintaining a vehicle for business purpose. Read more about [business mileage allowance](https://www.debitam.com/article/business-mileage-allowance/) in our article. These can be claimed by a sole trader or reimbursed to employees, as the case may be, at actual cost or [approved mileage rates](https://www.debitam.com/faq/).

These can be claimed by a sole trader or reimbursed to employees, as the case may be, at actual cost or approved mileage rates. 
*   What is a Tax Code?  Tax code is an alphanumeric code used to work out how much income tax needs to be collected from a person's income.These codes generally start with a number and end with an alphabet, for eg. 1250L. The number in a tax code is the amount of tax-free income the person gets in that particular year. The letter in the tax code refers to personal circumstances which may affect their [Personal Allowance](https://www.debitam.com/faq/). 
*   How does Employment and support allowance aid people with disabilities or health conditions?  [Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)](https://www.gov.uk/employment-support-allowance) is an allowance to help people with disability or health condition that affects how much you can work. Under ESA the government provides the applicants with either help in form of money to help with living costs in case they are unable to work or support to get back into work they can work. The applicant may be employed, self-employed or unemployed. 
*   Is Jobseeker's Allowance (JSA) an unemployment benefit provided by the UK government?  

[Jobseeker's Allowance (JSA)](https://www.gov.uk/jobseekers-allowance) is an unemployment benefit the UK government provides to people who are unemployed and actively seeking work. This allowance is provided to cover living expenses while the claimant is out of work. Payments of JSA Payments are usually made every 2 weeks. There are 3 types of Jobseeker’s Allowance (JSA):

    *   New style’ JSA
    *   Contribution-based JSA
    *   Income-based JSA 

Application for contribution-based and income-based JSA can be made only if the applicant either:

    *   gets the severe disability premium, or is entitled to it; or 
    *   got or was entitled to the severe disability premium within the last month and is still eligible for it. 

*   What is Child Tax Credit?  

[Child tax credit](https://www.gov.uk/child-tax-credit) is a benefit that a parent or carer gets if they are responsible for at least one child. This basically tops up the income of the one responsible for taking care of a child. It has been replaced by Universal Credit for most people. New claims for Child Tax Credit can be made if you:

    *   get the severe disability premium, or are entitled to it
    *   got or were entitled to the severe disability premium in the last month, and you're still eligible for it. 

It can be claimed until the September following your child's 16th birthday or if they are in approved education or training, you can claim until their 20th birthday. The amount of tax credit you receive depends on the number of children you have, and if your child has any disabilities and whether you are making a new claim for Child Tax Credit or already claiming Child Tax Credit.

*   What is Working Tax Credit?  

[Working Tax Credit](https://www.gov.uk/w


SOURCE: https://www.debitam.com/

## What do we do

You are already too busy with the daily grind as a business owner, what you need is an accountant who's prompt and reliable and can set you ready for the next step by making use of clear and business-focused language without paperwork faff, in other words, we do online accounting

It includes the preparation of [iXBRL accounts and tax returns](https://www.debitam.com/company-final-accounts-and-corporation-tax-ct600-return-filing-to-hmrc/).

## How it Works

In 4 easy and paperless steps;

![Image 22: Information sharing](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/home/how-it-works1.svg)
##### Information

 sharing

Your financial information is to be processed online in compliance with GDPR/EU. For example, you can share your bank statements via email with your accountant to walk through them.

![Image 23: Company Accounts & tax returns preparation](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/home/how-it-works2.svg)
##### Company Accounts &

tax returns preparation

iXBRL accounts and tax returns are prepared as per Companies House and HMRC's requirements.

![Image 24: Review & approval of drafts](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/home/how-it-works3.svg)
##### Review & approval 

 of drafts

You get to review and sign off on every step. We don't submit your accounts before having your approval.

![Image 25: Submission to HMRC & Companies House](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/home/how-it-works4.svg)
##### Submission to HMRC

& Companies House

Once you approve the accounts, we submit your company accounts & tax return to the authorities and share the proof of submission for your record.

[Watch Video](https://www.youtube.com/embed/aSjEotZSoEw)

## How long it takes

A [company account and tax return filing](https://www.debitam.com/company-final-accounts-and-corporation-tax-ct600-return-filing-to-hmrc/) for a limited company take us 3 to 5 working days in most circumstances.

If the deadline is fast approaching, we have an express company filing service that's guaranteed to submit your accounts to Companies House and HMRC within 24 hours for an extra £99.

# Mohit Baheti

### Co-founder And Director

Contact

Mohit Baheti is a seasoned professional with extensive experience in the accounting and IT sectors. As Co-Founder and COO of Acting Office since January 2023, Baheti has been instrumental in developing bespoke enterprise software tailored for accounting practices. Concurrently, Baheti co-founded Debitam, which serves over 25,000 UK companies with efficient account and tax filing services. In the role of General Partner at CapsiLabs, Baheti supports early-stage startups with funding and tech resources. Baheti also serves as a Director at Capsitech Limited, focusing on IT project management, including e-commerce and application development. Previous roles include Senior Chartered Accountant at Capium Limited, Manager at ICICI Prudential AMC Ltd, and Assistant Manager at Quantum Mutual Fund. Baheti holds multiple qualifications, including Chartered Accountant and Company Secretary, backed by a solid educational foundation in commerce.

Location

London, United Kingdom

Previous companies

[![Image 3: Quantum Mutual Fund logo](https://cdn.theorg.com/42c32785-b518-4241-a0be-884cfc85becd_thumb.jpg)](https://theorg.com/org/quantum-mutual-fund "Quantum Mutual Fund")[![Image 4: Capium Ltd logo](https://cdn.theorg.com/70cb70d7-cead-40cb-a979-92cdaf8de127_thumb.jpg)](https://theorg.com/org/capium-ltd "Capium Ltd")

Title: Debitam - Brand Profile - Endole
# Debitam - Brand Profile - Endole

[Endole](https://www.endole.co.uk/)[Endole App](https://app.endole.co.uk/)[Endole Open](https://open.endole.co.uk/)

[![Image 1: Endole Open](https://www.endole.co.uk/imgs/open-logo.svg)](https://open.endole.co.uk/)

![Image 2](https://open.endole.co.uk/imgs/brand.php?id=189227)

# Debitam

 Debitam is an online accounting and tax filing service that assists small businesses with their compliance duties. They provide a range of services for limited companies, sole traders, and freelancers. 

*   Payroll services
*   Tax planning advice
*   VAT & bookkeeping services
*   Confirmation statement services
*   Self-assessment tax return filing
*   Company accounts & tax return filing

* * *

Size

Small

Phone

020 3960 5080

Website

[debitam.com](https://debitam.com/)

Email

Available in Endole App

Locations

![Image 3](https://open.endole.co.uk/imgs/uk-map.svg)

42-44 Bishopsgate, EC2N 4AH

 02039605080 

 9:00 - 17:00

Harvest Accountants is a firm of Chartered Accountants and Bookkeepers that specialise in providing accounting and tax services to Sole Traders, Self-Employed Workers, Small Businesses, and Start-Ups.

![Image 7](https://open.endole.co.uk/imgs/brand.php?id=179411)

LeVeon Accounting Solutions is a dedicated online accounting service provider that offers bespoke solutions to help businesses grow for a low fixed monthly fee.

![Image 8](https://open.endole.co.uk/imgs/brand.php?id=262934)

[BH1 Accounting](https://open.endole.co.uk/insight/brand/262934-bh1-accounting)

BH1 Accounting is a premier accountancy and tax solutions provider based in London, offering services to sole traders, private limited companies, SME owners, and entrepreneurs.

Company Details

**Debitam is operated by Online Account Filing Limited** and is a Active company incorporated on 19 June 2018 with the registered office located in London, City of London. Online Account Filing Limited was registered 7 years ago.

# Dave Jangid

### Chairman & Founder

Contact

Dave Jangid is an accomplished entrepreneur and business leader, currently serving as the CEO and Founder of multiple ventures, including Acting Office, CapsiLabs, and Debitam - Online Account Filing, established between August 2022 and present, September 2018 and present, and June 2018 and present, respectively. The Online Account Filing service focuses on delivering efficient and cost-effective account and tax filing solutions for small businesses in the UK. As the CEO of Capsitech Limited since July 2009, Jangid oversees an IT company specializing in e-commerce development and software solutions, with an outsourcing office in Jodhpur, India. Previously, as Founder and CEO of Capium Limited from January 2012 to June 2018, Jangid developed online accounting and practice management software tailored for accountants. Prior experience includes serving as an Account Manager at McMillan Woods, London, from April 2006 to March 2012, managing a diverse client portfolio and providing financial advisory services. Jangid holds a Master's degree in Business Administration from the University of London and a Bachelor's degree in Business Administration from Jai Narain Vyas University.

Location

London, United Kingdom

Previous companies

In this episode, we dive into: 🔹 Why meetings are one of the biggest inefficiencies in accountancy firms 🔹 How Vinyl uses AI to automate notes, actions and client follow-ups 🔹 The role of AI in improving client experience and internal workflows 🔹 How firms are using Vinyl today to streamline onboarding, proposals and ongoing client communication If you’re looking to streamline your firm, elevate productivity, or understand where AI is genuinely adding value (not just hype), this is a must-listen. A huge thank you to Jordan for joining me — exciting to see how Vinyl is pushing the industry forward. Let me know your thoughts if you tune in!**

🚀 Starting your own business? Here’s something every young entrepreneur needs to hear. Our brilliant Account Manager, [Kerry Moore](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/kerry-moore-9a60161a3?trk=public_post-text), has seen it all — the wins, the worries, and the “wish I’d known that earlier” moments. Her biggest takeaway? 👉 Never underestimate the power of a great accountant. Because a good accountant doesn’t just “do your books.” They help you: 💡 See opportunities before they appear 💰 Avoid costly mistakes that can sink a business 📈 Build a strategy for growth — not just survival In Kerry’s words, “Having the right accountant is like having a financial co-pilot — someone who keeps you steady, informed, and ready for takeoff.” 🎥 Watch her share her three golden pieces of advice for every entrepreneur who wants to do more than just get by — they want to thrive. ##### **Transcript**

Kerry, give me 3 piece of advice that you would give small business owners. So #1 before you set up anything, it's a good idea to speak to an accountant. So not only can they help you set up like a limited company or being self-employed, but they can also guide you through the legal requirements. So you're not caught off guard #2 whether you should be set up as a limited company or self-employed. Different tax implications of filing, implications between the two. So if you speak to the accountant, they can guide you through which one might. Suit your business best. Lastly, if you're not sure, ask. We're here to help.

  People often see a founder’s success and think , “They must’ve taken a crazy risk.” What they don’t see are the months of research, the silent planning, the late night note taking, and the hundreds of “what if” scenarios mapped out before one decision was made. Starting a business isn’t a random leap; it’s a series of intentional steps taken with uncertain results. Yes, founders take risks , but not without calculation. Every bold move you see is usually backed by long nights of figuring out what could go wrong, and what could go right. The truth is, most founders aren’t chasing luck , they’re building it. At Founders Lab, we understand that behind every “big risk” is a founder who’s done the hard work to make it a smart one.   🚀 Is there ever a “right time” to start a business? At Elephants Child, we’ve seen that entrepreneurship looks very different at 25 than it does at 55 - but the core challenges remain the same. Steve Bird, Business Growth Adviser and founder of three successful technology ventures, explores how age and experience influence the journey - and why your mindset, motivation, and appetite for risk evolve over time. 💡 At a glance: • Younger founders are often driven by energy and discovery, while experienced founders tend to focus on purpose and clarity. • The Head (logical), Heart (emotional), and Hand (practical) framework can help balance creativity with strategic thinking. • Having an exit plan from the start brings focus, direction, and long-term value. As Steve puts it: “At 20, the goal is to arrive. At 50, the goal is to have a purpose and make a difference.” Whatever your age, the same principles hold true: build a clear vision, take time to plan strategically, and understand what success looks like for you - both personally and professionally. 👋 Whether you’re just starting out or scaling up, thoughtful planning today can create options for tomorrow.  Web Mention Index 

 This index measures how frequently a company or its website is mentioned across external sites, typically through referring domains. 5/5

Backlinks (links from other websites) show how well-known and trusted a company is online. However, the quality of these links is more significant than the quantity. This can indicate that the company is recognized as reliable and popular in its industry.

Title: Freshworks Doubles Revenue, Valuation, Raising $150M In Series H Funding
![Crunchbase News](https://news.crunchbase.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/cb_news_logo_gradient-2x.png)
![Crunchbase News](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAAAAACH5BAEKAAEALAAAAAABAAEAAAICTAEAOw==)

# Freshworks Doubles Revenue, Valuation, Raising $150M In Series H Funding

![](https://news.crunchbase.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/12/happy_unicorn_2-1.gif)
![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAAAAACH5BAEKAAEALAAAAAABAAEAAAICTAEAOw==) [Jason D. Rowley](https://news.crunchbase.com/author/jasonrowley/)
[jason\_rowley](http://www.twitter.com/jason_rowley)

Enterprise software is rarely exciting, but it provides the framework for doing things in the modern economy.

***Subscribe to the*** [***Crunchbase Daily***](https://news.crunchbase.com/daily/?utm_source=cb_news&utm_medium=article)

[Freshworks](https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/freshworks) is in the business of business software. Originally hailing from Chennai, India but now headquartered in San Mateo, California, the company develops and offers a suite of applications, ranging from its flagship help desk software Freshdesk, to IT ticket tracking tools, call center management software, and more.

Today, Freshwork announced that received a commitment of [$150 million for its Series H round](https://www.crunchbase.com/funding_round/freshworks-series-h--311b7719) which brings the company’s [total funding to $399 million](https://www.crunchbase.com/search/funding_rounds/field/organizations/funding_total/freshworks), according to Crunchbase data. The inside round was led by prior investors [Sequoia Capital](https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/sequoia-capital), [CapitalG](https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/google-capital), and longtime backer [Accel](https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/accel-partners). According to a statement from Freshworks, the transaction is expected to close by the end of the year, following completion of closing requirements and approval by U.S. antitrust regulators.

The deal values Freshworks at $3.5 billion, post-money, up 133 percent from the $1.5 billion valuation the company earned following [its $100 million Series G](https://www.crunchbase.com/funding_round/freshworks-series-g--840be151), closed a little over 15 months ago. Crunchbase News [covered that deal](https://news.crunchbase.com/news/freshworks-joins-the-unicorn-club-with-100m-series-g-co-led-by-sequoia-and-accel/) at the time. In June 2018, the company hired [Suresh Seshadri](https://www.crunchbase.com/person/suresh-seshadri) as its chief financial officer as it began solidifying plans to go public. With today’s funding announcement, Freshworks reaffirmed that it was on an “IPO track.”

Freshworks did not disclose additional information about its revenue. However, last year, CEO [Girish Mathrubootham](https://www.crunchbase.com/person/girish-mathrubootham) disclosed that the company had achieved an annual run rate of $100 million from over 150,000 customers. Earlier this week, Southeast Asia-focused tech publication [YourStory reported](https://yourstory.com/2019/11/freshworks-girish-mathrubootham-ipo-india-inc-saas-startup) that Freshworks now serves 220,000 clients, up nearly 47 percent from last year. Mathrubootham [told Indian startup publication Inc42](https://inc42.com/buzz/freshworks-bags-150-mn-from-google-others-a-step-closer-to-ipo/) that Freshworks now generates $200 million in ARR.

YourStory reported that Freshworks typically makes up the cost of acquiring a new customer after about 10 months of use, and that the average customer uses Freshworks software for four years. The publication also reports that the company earns about half of its revenue from mid-market and enterprise customers, with the remainder coming from small and medium-sized businesses.

The company’s path to IPO is long. The [Times of India reported](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/freshworks-edges-closer-to-a-nasdaq-listing/articleshow/70336769.cms), back in July, that Freshworks aimed to go public on the Nasdaq as soon as 2021. The paper reported that the company is hiring for senior manager of SEC reporting, indicating the company’s intent to compose and eventually submit an S-1 registration statement.

In the meantime, though the company is intent on expanding its footprint in the U.S. and other global markets. Freshworks currently has over 2,500 employees in offices around the world, including in Australia, the U.K., Singapore, France, the Netherlands, and India.

*Illustration: [Li-Anne Dias](http://www.li-anne.com/)*

○ Seed stage saw a total funding of $540M in H1 2024, a drop of 7% from $575M raised in H2 2023 and a drop of 43% raised in H1 2023. ○ Early stage witnessed a total funding of $3.2B in H1 2024, a drop of 12% from $3.7B raised in H1 2023 and similar amount of $3.2B was raised in H2 2023.
○ Late stage saw a total funding of $3B in H1 2024, a drop of 23% from $3.9B raised in H2 2023 and a growth of more than 30% raised in H1 2023.
Tracxn Insights (1/4) Geo Semi Annual Report - UK Tech - H1 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● London leads the space in terms of total funds raised in this H1 2024, followed by Cambridge and Oxford. ● H1 2024 has witnessed 12 100M+ funding rounds. Companies like Wayve, Abound and Monzo have managed to raise funds above $600M in this period.
○ Wayve has raised a total of $1.05B in Series C round led by SoftBank Group.
○ Abound raised $862M in Series B round.
○ Monzo raised $620M collectively in 2 Series I rounds.
● Enterprise Applications, FinTech and High Tech were the top-performing sectors in H1 2024 in this space.
○ Companies in the Enterprise Applications and FinTech segments raised around $2.7 billion each in H1 2024 and H1 2023. 5 Tracxn Insights (2/4) Geo Semi Annual Report - UK Tech - H1 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
6 ○ The High Tech space too, saw only a small change in funding, from $2 billion in H1 2023 to $2.3 billion in H1 2024.
● Mews was the only unicorn created in H1 2024 as compared to 2 unicorns in H1 2023.
● There were 195 companies acquired in H1 2024 which shows a dip as compared to 232 acquisitions in H1 2023 ○ Darktrace was acquired by Thoma Bravo at a price of $5.3B.
■ This becomes the highest valued acquisition in H1 2024 after the acquisition of EyeBio by Merck Sharp & Dohme at a price of $1.3B.
● Marex, AWAKN and Perfect Moment were the only companies that went public in H1 2024.
○ Three IPOs took place in H1 2023, and five in H2 2023 ● Octopus Ventures, Seedcamp and Parkwalk are the overall top investors in UK Tech ecosystem.
Tracxn Insights (3/4) Geo Semi Annual Report - UK Tech - H1 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
7 ● BGF, Y Combinator and Octopus Ventures are the overall top investors in UK Tech ecosystem for H1 2024.
○ Octopus Ventures, ACF and Molten Ventures are the overall top early-stage investors in UK Tech ecosystem for H1 2024.
○ CapitalG, BGF and Notion are the overall top late-stage investors in UK Tech ecosystem for H1 2024.
○ Techstars, Y Combinator and SOSV were the top seed-stage investors.

● France is the 7th highest funded country in 2024 ahead of Israel and Switzerland at positions 8th and 9th respectively. The top funded countries in this period are the United States, United Kingdom and China.
● A total of $5.6B was raised in 2024, a drop of 22% compared to $7.2B raised in 2023, and a drop of 52.64% compared to $11.75B raised in 2022.
○ Late Stage witnessed a total funding of $1.1B in 2024, a drop of 64.52% compared to $3.1B raised in 2023, and a drop of 83% compared to $6.5B raised in 2022.
○ Seed Stage saw a total funding of $606M in 2024, a drop of 5.61% compared to $642M raised in 2023, and a drop of 6.34% compared to $647 raised in 2022.
○ Early Stage saw a total funding of $3.9B in 2024, an increase of 11.43% compared to $3.5B raised in 2023, and a drop of 15.22% compared to $4.6B raised in 2022.
Tracxn Insights (1/5) 4 Geo Annual Report - France Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● In H2-2024, France Tech received $2.5B funding which is 20.42% lower than $3.1B in H1-2024, and 39.13% drop when compared to $4.1B raised in H2-2023.
● In Q4-2024, France Tech received $1.7B, which is a increase of 120.6% when compared to $769.6M in Q3-2024 and 11.42% less when compared to $1.9B in Q4-2023.
● 2024 has witnessed 10 100M+ funding rounds when compared to 13 such rounds in 2023 and 26 in 2022. ● Companies like Mistral AI, Poolside, Electra, H Company and Alan have managed to raise funds above $100M in this period.
○ Mistral AI has raised a total of $503M in a Series B round. ○ Poolside has raised a total of $500M in a Series B round led by Bain Capital Ventures valuing the company at over $3.0B. Tracxn Insights (2/5) 5 Geo Annual Report - France Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
○ Electra has raised a total of $333M in a Series B round led by PGGM valuing the company at over $304.67M.
● High Tech, Enterprise Applications, and FinTech were the top-performing sectors in 2024 in this space.
○ High Tech sector saw a total funding of $3.5B in 2024, which is a drop of 16% when compared to $4.1B raised in 2023 and a drop of 8% when compared to $3.8B raised in 2022.
○ Enterprise Applications saw a total funding of $3.0B in 2024, which is a increase of 12% when compared to $2.7B raised in 2023 and a drop of 53% when compared to $6.3M raised in 2022.
○ FinTech saw a total funding of $999.6M in 2024, which is a increase of 24% when compared to $807M raised in 2023 and a drop of 67% when compared to $3.0B raised in 2022.
● LightOn, Vaziva, ABL Diagnostics, and Macompta.fr were the companies that went public in 2024.
● There were 3 unicorns created in 2024, and 2 in 2023 Tracxn Insights (3/5) 6 Geo Annual Report - France Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● Tech companies in the France saw 129 acquisitions in 2024, which is a 18.4% decrease as compared to 158 acquisitions in 2023 and a drop by 38.8% when compared to 211 acquisitions in 2022.
● Paris based tech firms accounted for 68.32% of all funding seen by tech companies across the France. This was followed by Lyon at a distant second.
● Amolyt Pharma was acquired by Astrazeneca at a price of $1.1B.
○ This becomes the highest valued acquisition in 2024 after the acquisition of Sopra Banking Software by Axway at a price of $365M.
● Kima Ventures, Alven and Partech Partners were the overall top investors in France Tech ecosystem.
○ CIE IIITH, Kima Ventures and Y Combinator were the top seed-stage investors in France Tech ecosystem for 2024.
Tracxn Insights (4/5) 7 Geo Annual Report - France Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
○ DST Global, ICONIQ Growth and Leadblock Bitpanda Ventures were the top late-stage investors in France Tech ecosystem for 2024.
○ BNP Paribas Developpement, Partech Partners and Supernova Invest were the top early-stage investors in France Tech ecosystem for 2024.
● Among VCs, France based Kima Ventures led the most number of investments in 2024 with 7 rounds, while another France based fund Elaia added 11 new companies to its portfolio.
○ Late stage VC investments saw United Kingdom based DST Global and United States based ICONIQ Growth add 5 and 4 companies each to their portfolios.

Title: Japan Tech Annual Funding Report - 2024
TRACXN GEO ANNUAL REPORT JAPAN TECH - 2024 Geo Annual Report - Japan Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
Snapshot Investments Investors Exits New Unicorns Citywise Trends News Funded Deadpooled Companies Appendix Contents Geo Annual Report - Japan Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
Snapshot Snapshot Investments Investors Exits New Unicorns Citywise Trends News Funded Deadpooled Companies Appendix Contents Geo Annual Report - Japan Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● The Japanese Tech startup ecosystem experienced peak funding in 2021 and started to witness a moderate decline since then. However, funding has seen a recovery in 2024 compared to the funds witnessed in 2023 ○ In 2024, the sector witnessed a 15% increase in funding compared to 2023 although it was a 5% decline from funds raised in 2022 ○ Despite macroeconomic challenges and geopolitical uncertainties have impacted the economy, the investments have remained comparatively stable in the past 5 years, but the growth has been sluggish ○ Japan is among the top 15 countries globally in terms of investments attracted this year ● Japan has faced certain unique challenges toward innovation over the past few decades ○ Factors such as the slow adoption of emerging technologies, a shortage of skilled IT professionals due to demographic shifts, and linguistic barriers limiting global collaboration have hindered progress Tracxn Insights (1/6) 4 Geo Annual Report - Japan Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● To counter these challenges, the Japanese government has introduced several initiatives to support the sector's growth ○ To revive its semiconductor industry and address global chip shortage, Japan has ramped up its efforts to support the sector. A sum of $4.86B has been pledged to the semiconductor giant TSMC in the form of subsidies to build a second fabrication plant in the country. TSMC has ramped up its production in the country in recent times. The government has also planned to invest $635M in Rapidus, a Japanese chip manufacturer, that intends to start trial production of chips in April and mass production by 2027 ○ Additionally, Japan has partnered with U.S. chip startup Tenstorrent to train 200 Japanese chip designers over five years, strengthening the country’s semiconductor capabilities ○ The government has also made efforts to support the budding startup ecosystem through initiatives such as the J-Startup program for providing support to startups, the provision of a Startup Visa to support entrepreneurs coming to Japan, among others Tracxn Insights (2/6) 5 Geo Annual Report - Japan Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● Despite current demographic challenges, Japan’s aging society presents a unique opportunity to innovate in healthcare and robotics. By developing technologies tailored to an older population, Japan can be a global leader in age-related tech solutions. A focus on AI-enabled automation and agentic AI can be beneficial towards tackling labor shortages ● Japan Tech witnessed a total of $2.1B in 2024, an increase of 15% compared to $1.8B witnessed in 2023, but a decline of 5% was seen compared to $2.2B witnessed in 2022 ○ 314 funding rounds were seen in 2024, an increase of 9% compared to 289 funding rounds in 2023 and a rise of 19% compared to 264 rounds in 2022 ○ Seed stage rounds witnessed a total of $212M in 2024, a growth of 112% compared to $100M raised in 2023 and an increase of 36% compared to $156M in 2022 ○ Early-stage rounds witnessed $1.1B in 2024, reflecting a 10% increase from $1B in 2023 but an 8% decline compared to $1.2B in 2022 ○ Late-stage rounds witnessed $743M in 2024, reflecting a 10% increase compared to $678M witnessed in 2023 but a 15% decline compared to $872M in 2022 Tracxn Insights (3/6) 6 Geo Annual Report - Japan Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● The Japan Tech sector saw 3 $100M+ rounds in 2024 an increase of 50% compared to 2 rounds in 2023 ○ Sakana, a Tokyo-based Artificial Intelligence research company, raised $344M in multiple funding rounds and was the highest-funded company in 2024 in this region ○ Smart HR, a platform that offers HR management solutions, raised $140M in its series E funding round ● Enterprise Applications, Environment Tech, and Auto Tech were the top funded segments in this space in 2024 ○ The Enterprise Applications segment has witnessed a total of $1.05B in 2024, this is a growth of 77% compared to $592M raised in 2023 and a rise of 30% compared to $806M raised in 2022. ■ This space accounts for 53.3% of the funding witnessed in this region in 2024 ○ The Environment Tech segment witnessed a total of $221M in 2024, a growth of 51% compared to $147M raised in 2023 and a rise of 52% compared to $146M raised in 2022 Tracxn Insights (4/6) 7 Geo Annual Report - Japan Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
○ The Auto Tech segment witnessed a total of $204M in 2024, a growth of 115% compared to $95M raised in 2023 and a 6% rise as compared to $193M raised in 2022 ● The Japanese tech ecosystem witnessed only 1 unicorn in 2024 ○ Sakana, a Tokyo-based artificial intelligence research company, raised $214M in a Series A funding round with a valuation of $1.5B ● Japan's Tech sector in 2024 saw 21 acquisitions, which is a 17% increase from 18 acquisitions in 2023 ○ Coin Master, a cryptocurrency exchange platform, was acquired by Gate.io ○ KADOKAWA, an Ebook library & reading platform, was acquired by Sony ● 29 companies went public in this space in 2024, a 53% increase compared to 19 companies that went public in 2023 ● Tokyo leads the funding in Japan Tech space, followed by Minato City and Shibuya Tracxn Insights (5/6) 8 Geo Annual Report - Japan Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● Mitsubishi UFJ Capital, SMBC Venture Capital, and Global Brain are the overall top investors in the Japanese tech sector ○ Global Brain, Incubate Fund, and Deepcore were the top investors in Seed-stage rounds in 2024 ○ SMBC Venture Capital, Nissay Capital, and Mitsubishi UFJ Capital were the top investors in the Early-stage rounds of 2024 ○ Cygames Capital, Niigata Venture Capital, and JBC were the top investors in Late-stage rounds in 2024 Tracxn Insights (6/6) 9 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.

● Vietnam is the 51st highest funded country in 2024 ahead of Iceland and Luxembourg at positions 52nd and 53rd respectively. The top funded countries in this period are the United States, United Kingdom and China.
● A total of $120M was raised in 2024, a drop of 38% compared to $196M raised in 2023, and a drop of 77% compared to $518M raised in 2022.
○ Late Stage witnessed a total funding of $14.2M in 2024.
○ Seed Stage saw a total funding of $11.7M in 2024, a drop of 32.37% compared to $17.3M raised in 2023, and a drop of 81.97% compared to $64.9M raised in 2022.
○ Early Stage saw a total funding of $94.4M in 2024, a drop of 47.26% compared to $179M raised in 2023, and a drop of 79.16% compared to $453M raised in 2022.
● In H2-2024, Vietnam Tech received $53.6M funding which is 19.63% lower than $66.7M in H1-2024, and is 41.68% drop when compared to $91.9M raised in H2-2023.
Tracxn Insights (1/4) 4 Geo Annual Report - Vietnam Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● In Q4-2024, Vietnam Tech received $35.1M that is an increase of 89.73% when compared to $18.5M in Q3-2024 and 68.75% rise when compared to $20.8M in Q4-2023.
● No companies raised funds above $100M in 2024.
● Gig Economy, Auto Tech, and Transportation and Logistics Tech were the top-performing sectors in 2024 in this space.
○ Gig Economy sector saw a total funding of $38.2M in 2024 which is a increase of 3715% when compared to $1M raised in 2023 and a rise of 381% when compared to $7.9M raised in 2022.
○ Auto Tech sector saw a total funding of $35.3M in 2024 which is a increase of 1078% when compared to $3M raised in 2023 and a rise of 116% when compared to $16.4M raised in 2022.
○ Transportation and Logistics Tech sector saw a total funding of $34.3M in 2024 which is a increase of 1045% when compared to $3M raised in 2023 and a rise of 58% when compared to $21.8M raised in 2022.
Tracxn Insights (2/4) 5 Geo Annual Report - Vietnam Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● No companies went public in 2024.
● No unicorns were created in 2024 and 2023.
● Tech companies in the Vietnam saw 3 acquisitions in 2024, which is a 25% decrease as compared to 4 acquisitions in 2023 and a drop by 40% when compared to 5 acquisitions in 2022.
● Ho Chi Minh City based tech firms accounted for 56.59% of all funding seen by tech companies across the Vietnam. This was followed by Hanoi at a distant second.
● RHB Vietnam Securities was acquired by Public Bank at a price of $15.2M.
○ This becomes the highest valued acquisition in 2024 after the acquisition of VinBrain by NVIDIA.
● CyberAgent Capital, 500 Global and Genesia Ventures were the overall top investors in Vietnam Tech ecosystem.
○ Antler, Monk's Hill Ventures and Techstars were the top seed-stage investors in Vietnam Tech ecosystem for 2024.
Tracxn Insights (3/4) 6 Geo Annual Report - Vietnam Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
○ Vertex Ventures, Mitsubishi UFJ Capital and Inspire were the top early-stage investors in Vietnam Tech ecosystem for 2024.
● Among VCs, United States based Wavemaker Partners led the most number of investments in 2024 with 15 rounds, while United Kingdom based fund Northstar Ventures added 8 new companies to its portfolio.

4 Tracxn Insights (1/1) ● The UAE Tech startup ecosystem has raised a total of $234M in H1 2024 till date. It is a 62% drop in funding compared to H2 2023 and 47% drop from H1 2023.
○ Seed stage saw a total funding of $83M in H1 2024 till date, a drop of 50.6% from $168M raised in H2 2023 and a drop of 23.5% raised in H1 2023.
○ Early stage witnessed a total funding of $151M in H1 2024 till date, a drop of 13% from $174M raised in H2 2023 and an increase of 20.6% raised in H1 2023.
○ No funding raised in late-stage H1 2024 which is a drop as compared to $275M and $208M raised in late-stage H2 2023 and late-stage H1 2023 respectively.
● Dubai leads the space in terms of total funds raised in this H1 2024, followed by Abu Dhabi.
● Enterprise Applications, FinTech and Real Estate & Construction Tech were the top-performing sectors in H1 2024 in this space.
Geo Semi Annual Report - UAE Tech - H1 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● There were 9 acquisitions in H1 2024 which is a 25% decline compared to 12 acquisitions in H1 2023.
○ Drive Arabia, AIQ and Callmi are some of the companies that got acquired in H1 2024.
● Alef Education was the only company to go public in H1 2024.
● Wamda Capital, 500 Global and Middle East Venture Partners are the overall top investors in UAE Tech ecosystem.
● Oryx Fund, Middle East Venture Partners and Oraseya Capital are the overall top investors in UAE Tech ecosystem for H1 2024.
○ Elevation Capital, Balderton Capital and Jump Capital are the overall top early-stage investors in UAE Tech ecosystem for H1 2024.

Title: Singapore Tech Annual Funding Report - 2024
TRACXN GEO ANNUAL REPORT SINGAPORE TECH - 2024 Geo Annual Report - Singapore Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
Snapshot Investments Investors Exits New Unicorns Citywise Trends News Funded Deadpooled Companies Appendix Contents Geo Annual Report - Singapore Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
Snapshot Snapshot Investments Investors Exits New Unicorns Citywise Trends News Funded Deadpooled Companies Appendix Contents Geo Annual Report - Singapore Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● Singapore is the 11th highest funded country in 2024 ahead of Japan and Australia at positions 12th and 13th respectively. The top funded countries in this period are the United States, United Kingdom and China.
● A total of $2.1B was raised in 2024, a drop of 56% compared to $4.8B raised in 2023, and a drop of 74% compared to $8.1B raised in 2022.
○ Late Stage witnessed a total funding of $708M in 2024, a drop of 74.71% compared to $2.8B raised in 2023, and a drop of 82.73% compared to $4.1B raised in 2022.
○ Seed Stage saw a total funding of $310M in 2024, a drop of 50.6% compared to $627M raised in 2023, and a drop of 63.28% compared to $844M raised in 2022.
○ Early Stage saw a total funding of $1.1B in 2024, a drop of 15.38% compared to $1.3B raised in 2023, and a drop of 65.63% compared to $3.2B raised in 2022.
Tracxn Insights (1/5) 4 Geo Annual Report - Singapore Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● In H2-2024, Singapore Tech received $939.2M funding that is a drop of 20.85% when compared to $1.2B in H1-2024, and 32.86% drop when compared to $1.4B raised in H2-2023.
● In Q4-2024, Singapore Tech received $540.5M that is a increase of 35.55% when compared to $398.7M in Q3-2024 and 38.39% drop when compared to $877.2M in Q4-2023.
● 2024 has witnessed 3 100M+ funding rounds when compared to 7 such rounds in 2023 and 19 in 2022.
● Companies like Eruditus, ANEXT Bank and Bolttech have managed to raise funds above $100M in this quarter.
○ Eruditus has raised a total of $150M in a Series F round led by The Rise Fund valuing the company at over $3.1B.
○ ANEXT Bank has raised a total of $148M in a Series D round led by Ant Group.
Tracxn Insights (2/5) 5 Geo Annual Report - Singapore Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
○ Bolttech has raised a total of $100M in a Series C round led by Dragon Funds valuing the company at over $2.1B.
● A major part of these $100M+ funding rounds are from FinTech, EdTech, and High Tech.
● FinTech, High Tech, and Enterprise Applications were the top-performing sectors in 2024 in this space.
○ FinTech sector saw a total funding of $1B in 2024 which is a drop of 15% when compared to $1.2B raised in 2023 and a drop of 71% when compared to $3.5B raised in 2022.
○ HighTech saw a total funding of $884M in 2024 which is a increase of 4% when compared to $848M raised in 2023 and a drop of 67% when compared to $2.6B raised in 2022.
○ Enterprise Applications saw a total funding of $597M in 2024 which is a drop of 29% when compared to $840M in 2023 and a drop of 77% when compared to $2.6B raised in 2022.
Tracxn Insights (3/5) 6 Geo Annual Report - Singapore Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
● Kioxia, MaNaDr and RYDE were the companies that went public in 2024.
● There was only one unicorn created in 2024, while there were no unicorns created in 2023.
● Tech companies in the Singapore saw 39 acquisitions in 2024, which is a 2% decrease as compared to 40 acquisitions in 2023, and a drop of 19% when compared to 48 acquisitions in 2022.
● PropertyGuru was acquired by EQT at a price of $1.1B.
○ This becomes the highest valued acquisition in 2024 after the acquisition of Skuad by Payoneer at a price of $61M.
● Wavemaker Partners,Peak XV Partners and SEEDS Capital were the overall top investors in Singapore Tech ecosystem.
○ Antler, MH Ventures and Waterdrip Capital were the top seed-stage investors in Singapore Tech ecosystem for 2024.
Tracxn Insights (4/5) 7 Geo Annual Report - Singapore Tech - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.
○ SoftBank Vision Fund, NewView Capital and The Rise Fund were the top late-stage investors in Singapore Tech ecosystem for 2024.
○ SEEDS Capital, Peak XV Partners and UOB were the top early-stage investors in Singapore Tech ecosystem for 2024.
● Among VCs, United Kingdom based MH Ventures led the most number of investments in 2024 with 6 rounds, while Singapore based fund HashKey Capital added 9 new companies to its portfolio.
○ Late stage VC investments saw United Kingdom based SoftBank Vision Fund and United States based NewView Capital add 2 and 2 companies each to their portfolios.
Tracxn Insights (5/5) 8 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved.

Title: Aviso Raises $15M in Series B Funding at $55M Valuation | PitchBook
Title: Aviso Raises $15M in Series B Funding at $55M Valuation | PitchBook
# Aviso Raises $15M in Series B Funding at $55M Valuation. Aviso, a predictive insights software company for sales management, operations and forecasting, has raised $15 million in Series B funding co-led by Scale Venture Partners and Next World Capital. All existing angels and investors participated in the round, including Shasta Ventures, First Round Capital, Bloomberg Beta and Cowboy Ventures. The investment gives the company a $55 million post-money valuation. Aviso previously raised $8 million in Series A funding at a valuation of $33 million last April. Join the more than 1.5 million industry professionals who get our daily newsletter! ## Related content.

## About DEBITAM INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED

DEBITAM INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED is a Private Limited Company and was incorporated on Dec 08, 2025 in India.It is registered at Registrar of Companies, ROC Jaipur.Its registered address is H 298-299 3RD PHASE RICCO, INDUSTRIAL AREA, BORANADA, Jodhpur, Jodhpur, Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India, 342012. Its corporate identification number is (CIN) U69200RJ2025FTC109349 and its current status is Active.

DEBITAM INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED's authorised share capital is Rs. 500,000 and its paid up capital is Rs. 100,000.The current board of directors of DEBITAM INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED comprises[Anand Vardhan Soni](https://platform.tracxn.com/a/d/people/6957e2e6b5647f061fae30c6/anandvardhansoni) and [Mohit Baheti](https://platform.tracxn.com/a/d/people/688c2a663e9bbe64632231d7/mohitbaheti).

# Contents Geo Annual Report - FinTech - UK - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved. 

● The UK’s FinTech Startup ecosystem ranks as the second most funded ecosystem globally in the FinTech space, after the US, in 2024. 

○ The region witnessed its peak funding in 2021, after which the funds started to decline steadily and 2024 became the least funded year in the past five years. 

■ This decline can be attributed to several factors. The evolving regulatory landscape has introduced stricter compliance requirements, particularly concerning digital assets and online payments, which has created uncertainties for investors. 

■ Concerns over fraud and cybersecurity issues have heightened scrutiny on Fintech firms, making investors more cautious. 

■ Apart from this, macroeconomic challenges, including high inflation rates and rising interest rates, have increased borrowing costs for consumers and businesses alike, impacting spending and investment in FinTech solutions. 

4

# Tracxn Insights (1/8) Geo Annual Report - FinTech - UK - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved. 

● The UK government has implemented a comprehensive suite of measures to bolster the nation's FinTech ecosystem, including regulatory reforms, innovation accelerators, and funding initiatives. 

○ The UK has driven policy-led innovation globally by funding key initiatives such as the creation of the Financial Conduct Authority's (FCA) Regulatory Sandbox, the Global Financial Innovation Network (GFIN), the Open Banking framework, and the AI Sector Deal, setting a global benchmark. 

■ The FCA has implemented a scheme called Innovation Hub to reduce regulatory burdens on fintech companies. 

■ The FinTech Pledge demonstrates the government and industry's commitment to the growth of the UK's FinTech sector by establishing bank-fintech partnerships that are efficient and transparent. 

5

# Tracxn Insights (2/8) Geo Annual Report - FinTech - UK - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved. 

○ The government is preparing a financial services growth and competitiveness strategy and will publish it in spring 2025 to strengthen the sector's competitiveness and sustainability. 

● In 2024, UK’s FinTech ecosystem has witnessed a total of $4.9B in funding, a 2% and 57% drop as compared to the funding raised in 2023 and 2022, which saw $5B and $11.5B in total funding, respectively. 

○ Late-stage funding rounds witnessed a total funding of $2.1B in 2024, experiencing a drop of 13% and 68% compared to the $2.4B and $6.5B raised in 2023 and 2022 respectively. 

○ Early-stage funding in 2024 saw a funding of $2.5B, a 19% raise compared to the $2.1B funding raised in 2023 and a decline of 40% compared to the $4.2B raised in 2022. 

○ Seed-stage funding in 2024 witnessed $265M in total funding, a decline of 43% and 67% compared to $467M and $798M raised in 2023 and 2022, respectively. 

6

# Tracxn Insights (3/8) Geo Annual Report - FinTech - UK - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved. 

○ Q2 2024 has witnessed the major part of funds raised in 2024 with overall funding of $2.08B, contributing to more than 42% of the total funds raised in 2024 and Q3 was the least funded quarter of the year. 

● Alternative Lending, Banking Tech and Payments were the top performing segments in the UK’s FinTech ecosystem in 2024. 

○ Alternative Lending segment witnessed a funding of $1.53B in 2024, a 67% increase compared to that of $918M received in 2023 and a 3% increase in comparison with $1.48B raised in 2022. 

■ Abound, a provider of an online platform for personal loans, has received $1B in Series B funding led by GSR Ventures and Hambro Perks. It also accounts for 65% of the total funding raised in Alternative Lending space. 

7

# Tracxn Insights (4/8) Geo Annual Report - FinTech - UK - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved. 

○ Banking Tech segment has witnessed a total funding of $1.21B in 2024, a 28% increase compared to $944M received in 2023 and a 41% drop compared to $2.04B raised in 2022. 

○ Payments segment saw a total funding of $621M in 2024, a 25% and 81% drop in comparison to that of $832M and $3.21B raised in 2023 and 2022, respectively. 

● Digital Remittance and Forex Tech are the worst-performing segments in 2024, based on funding to date. The Digital Remittance segment experienced a decline of 86%, while Forex Tech saw a 55% drop in funding compared to the amounts raised in 2023. 

● The UK’s FinTech space saw 8, $100M+ funding rounds in 2024 compared to 10 and 24 rounds in the year 2023 and 2022, resulting in a drop of 20% and 67%, respectively. 

○ Abound, an online platform for personal loans has raised the highest sum of $1.0B in a Series B funding round. 

8

# Tracxn Insights (5/8) Geo Annual Report - FinTech - UK - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved. 

○ Monzo, a digital bank for individuals and businesses, has raised $191M in Series I funding round and has achieved a post money valuation of $5.9B. 

○ Zepz which offers money transfer services, has raised $267M in a series F funding round. 

● The UK’s FinTech startup ecosystem witnessed only one unicorn in 2024 as compared to 2 in 2023 and 4 in 2022. 

○ MoniePoint, provider of SME loans, raised $110M in its latest series C funding round with a valuation of $1B. 

● The UK’s FinTech sector observed 2 IPOs in 2024, a 100% increase compared to 1 each in 2023 and 2022, respectively. 

○ Marex, an online platform specializing in tech-enabled liquidity solutions, went public in April 2024 with market capitalization of $1.35B. 

9

# Tracxn Insights (6/8) Geo Annual Report - FinTech - UK - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved. 

○ Diasoft, provider of software solutions for BFSI sector, went public in February 2024. 

● UK’s FinTech startup ecosystem saw 87 acquisitions in 2024, a rise of 8% as compared to 81 acquisitions witnessed in 2023. 

○ Preqin, provider of data, insights, and tools for the alternative assets industry, was acquired by BlackRock at acquisition price of $3.22B. This is the highest valued acquisition in 2024. 

● London leads the funding in UK’s FinTech space, followed by Andover and Maidstone. 

○ Over the past five years, London has consistently secured the highest amount of funding in the UK’s FinTech sector. It now stands as the third-largest funded city in this industry on a global scale after Bay Area and San Francisco. 

○ 94% of the funds raised in UK FinTech space is from London. 

10 

# Tracxn Insights (7/8) Geo Annual Report - FinTech - UK - 2024 Copyright © 2024, Tracxn Technologies Limited. All rights reserved. 

● Seedcamp, Techstars and Anthemis Group are the overall top investors in the UK FinTech Startup space. 

○ CIE IIITH, Y Combinator and We Founder Circle are the lead investors in the seed-stage in 2024. 

○ CommerzVentures, Fidelity International Strategic Ventures and AlbionVC are the top early-stage investors in 2024. 

○ TCV, CapitalG and Sprints are the most active investors in the late-stage in 2024. 

11

## Debitam - Online Account FilingEmail Formats

Debitam - Online Account Filinguses 3 email formats. The most common is {first name}.{last name} (e.g., john.doe@debitam.com), used 56.8% of the time.

| Format | Example | Percentage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| {first name}.{last name} | john.doe@debitam.com | 56.8% |
| {first initial}.{last name} | j.doe@debitam.com | 32.4% |
| {first name} | john@debitam.com | 10.8% |

## About Debitam - Online Account Filing

Online Accounting Service | Bookkeeping | Payroll | Personal Tax Return Filing to HMRC | Corporation Tax Filing | HMRC ONLINE FILING: Our aim is to help small business owners` tax return and account filing compliance works by using cutting-edge technology and adopting a versatile corporate culture. WHO ARE WE: We are a web-based online filing service on which you can find real people, who are qualified accountants, behind it. WHY US: Online Account Filing distinct from its peers with its exclusively assigned accountant system to each client and its significantly lower prices. As much as being a digital platform, we also offer a wide range of tax advice during your filing journey with us. REVIEWS: Online Account Filing is the top accountancy firm on Trust Pilot for 5 months in a row with over 400 Excellent rated reviews and 4.9 Trust Score. LOCATION: Our HQ is one of the well-connected locations in Bishopsgate, London. SERVICES: - Company Accounts & Tax Return - Self-Assessment Tax Return - VAT & Bookkeeping services, by adjusting ourselves with your previous accounting application. - CIS compliance works - Payroll running - Confirmation Statement - Assisting and reminding your businesses tax return and company filing deadlines to help you to avoid missing any deadline with your businesses tax returns. KNOWLEDGE: Visit our Knowledge section to update yourself with the most recent developments in Taxation, Business Loans, Tax Claims and Small Business Tips: >>> https://www.onlineaccountfiling.com/blog/

# Private Limited Company Examples: Your Complete Guide

![Dave Jangid | Debitam](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/authors/dave-jangid60x60.webp)
![Share to Facebook](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/social/facebook.svg)
![Share to Twitter](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/social/twitter.svg)
![Share to Linkedin](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/social/linkedin.svg)
![Share to Whatsapp](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/themes/debitam-uk/image/social/whatsapp.svg)
![Private Limited Company Examples in the UK | Debitam Online Account Filing](https://www.debitam.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/private-limited-company-examples-in-the-uk.jpg)

Choosing the right structure for your business is a crucial decision for any entrepreneur. Do you need flexibility, or is protecting your personal assets your top priority? A private limited company (Ltd) is a popular choice for many UK businesses, offering a balance of both.

This guide explains what a private limited company is and provides real-world **examples of private limited companies**. It also highlights the key differences between Ltds, public limited companies (PLCs), and limited liability partnerships (LLPs). By the end, you'll have a clearer understanding of whether an Ltd is the right choice for your business.

## What Is a Private Limited Company?

A private limited company is a business structure that exists as a separate legal entity from its owners. Essentially, it functions as its own legal person—it can own property, sign contracts, and be sued in its own name.

This separation is crucial, as it creates a "veil" between the business and its shareholders. The key advantages include:

Setting up a private limited company means registering with Companies House and appointing at least one director. While this involves more administrative work than being a sole trader, the protection and credibility gained are significant.

## How Is an Ltd Different from Other Business Structures?

It's easy to get lost in the alphabet soup of business acronyms. Let's clarify how a private limited company (Ltd) stands apart from other common structures.

### Private Limited Company (Ltd) vs. Public Limited Company (PLC)

The main difference lies in ownership and regulation.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Feature** | **Private Limited Company (Ltd)** | **Public Limited Company (PLC)** |
| **Share Ownership** | Shares are sold privately and cannot be offered to the general public. | Shares can be traded publicly on a stock exchange, like the London Stock Exchange. |
| **Share Capital** | There is no minimum share capital requirement. | Must have a minimum share capital of £50,000, with at least 25% paid up. |
| **Directors** | Requires at least one director. | Requires a minimum of two directors. |
| **Company Secretary** | Appointing a company secretary is optional. | Must have at least one qualified company secretary. |
| **Regulation** | Fewer reporting requirements and more privacy. | Stricter financial reporting rules and greater public scrutiny. |

A PLC structure suits large companies looking for significant public investment. In contrast, an Ltd offers greater control and privacy, making it perfect for most startups and small to medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

### Private Limited Company (Ltd) vs. Limited Liability Partnership (LLP)

Both an Ltd and an LLP offer limited liability, yet their internal management and tax treatment are quite distinct.

LLPs are a common choice for professional services firms, such as solicitors or accountants. In contrast, Ltds are a more conventional corporate structure suited to a wider range of commercial businesses.

## What Are Examples of a Private Limited Company?

Ever wondered what a private limited company (Ltd) looks like in the real world? Many of the UK's biggest and most familiar brands operate as Ltds, even though they're household names. They manage to grow significantly while keeping ownership private.

Let's look at a few Ltd examples you might already know:

These examples show how a private limited company structure can support significant growth and innovation.

You might be surprised to learn how many well-known businesses are not publicly traded. In the UK, many familiar brands operate as private limited companies.

Here are a few more notable examples of private limited companies from the UK:

Even global giants like IKEA and Rolex operate as private entities, demonstrating that you don't need to go public to achieve worldwide success.

### Is Aer Lingus a Private Limited Company?

That's a great question, and the answer actually tells a fascinating story about how a company's structure can change!

Aer Lingus started its journey as Ireland's state-owned airline. Then, it evolved into a public limited company (PLC), with its shares traded on both the Irish and London Stock Exchanges.

But here's where it gets interesting: in 2015, International Airlines Group (IAG), the same company behind British Airways, acquired Aer Lingus.

**What happened then?** It was de-listed from the stock markets. So, while IAG is publicly traded, Aer Lingus Group DAC now operates as a registered private company, essentially a subsidiary.

This journey perfectly illustrates how a company's legal and ownership structure isn't static; it can shift significantly over time due to acquisitions and strategic decisions. Did you know a company could go through so many changes?

## Ready to Start Your Business Journey?

Choosing your business structure is a foundational step. A private limited company offers key benefits like liability protection, credibility, and control.

Feeling inspired by these examples? Ready to build your dream business?

To protect platform integrity, every review on our platform—verified or not—is screened by our 24/7 automated software. This technology is designed to identify and remove content that breaches our guidelines, including reviews that are not based on a genuine experience. We recognise we may not catch everything, and you can flag anything you think we may have missed.
