- **💼 𝐈𝐬 𝐘𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐁𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐈𝐝𝐞𝐚 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐡 𝐈𝐭? 𝐔𝐬𝐞 𝐍𝐏𝐕 𝐭𝐨 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐝 𝐎𝐮𝐭!📊 𝐍𝐏𝐕 (𝐍𝐞𝐭 𝐏𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐕𝐚𝐥𝐮𝐞) tells you if the money you’ll earn in the future is really worth more than what you’re investing today. Thinking about launching a gift shop, a side hustle, or even a new product? Here’s how NPV can help you make a smarter financial decision — not just based on gut instinct, but on numbers that talk. 💡 🛍 Scenario 1: Handmade Gift Shop 🏗 Initial Investment: ₹50,000 📈 Expected Profits: • Year 1 → ₹15,000 • Year 2 → ₹18,000 • Year 3 → ₹20,000 📉 Discount Rate: 10% 🔢 NPV Calculation: PV of future profits = ₹43,539 NPV = 43,539 – 50,000 = –₹6,461 👉 Negative NPV → Not financially viable (in this setup). 🛍 Scenario 2: With Revised Inflows 🏗 Initial Investment: ₹50,000 📈 Expected Profits: • Year 1 → ₹18,000 • Year 2 → ₹22,000 • Year 3 → ₹25,000 📉 Discount Rate: 10% 🔢 NPV Calculation: PV of future profits = ₹53,328 NPV = 53,328 – 50,000 = +₹3,328 👉 Positive NPV → Project adds value. Go for it! 🚀 📌 𝑾𝒉𝒂𝒕 𝑻𝒉𝒊𝒔 𝑴𝒆𝒂𝒏𝒔 ✅ NPV tells you whether a business idea is worth investing in or not. ✅ But to calculate the right NPV, your estimates must be realistic and aligned with the real world. That’s where tools like strategic costing, trend analysis, and market research come into play. They make your assumptions stronger — and your financial decisions smarter. (I’ll dive deeper into trend analysis in my next post — stay tuned 😉) 💬 Whether you’re a CMA, entrepreneur, or simply someone who wants to make better financial decisions — NPV provides a realistic, comprehensive, and value-based decision framework — rooted in financial logic. 📊 It’s not just for big corporations. It’s for anyone making decisions today that impact tomorrow. #Finance #Entrepreneurship #CMA #SmallBusiness #NPV #CostAccounting #FinancialDecisions #BusinessStrategy**  
Aishwarya Suthar liked this  
[View Post](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/finance-entrepreneurship-cma-activity-7365323111749087232-6ELm)  
![Activity Image](https://media.licdn.com/dms/image/v2/D5622AQHR5YOO-ujueg/feedshare-shrink_800/B56ZjbiVBiIEAg-/0/1756029869842?e=2147483647&v=beta&t=wTs__A25YRgxiSMed1pby_rtSIogu-LtO_4KWHV3cIM)

- **𝑻𝒓𝒆𝒏𝒅 𝑨𝒏𝒂𝒍𝒚𝒔𝒊𝒔: 𝑳𝒐𝒐𝒌𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝑩𝒆𝒚𝒐𝒏𝒅 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝑵𝒖𝒎𝒃𝒆𝒓𝒔 📈 In my last post, we saw how NPV helps decide whether a business idea is worth pursuing. But here’s the catch, the accuracy of the NPV calculation depends heavily on the quality of the projections. To increase the reliability of your NPV analysis, one can employ techniques that refine your estimates and account for uncertainty. 👉 That’s where Trend Analysis comes in. By studying how revenues, costs, or customer demand have moved over time, we can make realistic forecasts instead of guesses. If you only assume profits will grow every year, your NPV may look attractive. But… what if sales dip during off-seasons? Or raw material costs rise faster than expected? 𝑾𝒊𝒕𝒉 𝑻𝒓𝒆𝒏𝒅 𝑨𝒏𝒂𝒍𝒚𝒔𝒊𝒔, 𝒚𝒐𝒖 𝒄𝒂𝒏: ▪️ Spot seasonal patterns ▪️ Predict cost changes ▪️ Identify realistic growth rates ▪️ Learn from the past to forecast the future In fact, most of us use this technique in real life without even realizing it. ➡️ A clear example is when we make budgets for ourselves. We look at how we spent in the past, and then predict what we’ll need for the coming months. 📌 𝑲𝒆𝒚 𝑻𝒂𝒌𝒆𝒂𝒘𝒂𝒚𝒔 ▪️NPV tells you if a business is worth it. ▪️Trend Analysis ensures your assumptions reflect reality. Together, they make your financial decisions smarter, sharper and more realistic. Have you used trend analysis in your work or personal life? #Finance #Entrepreneurship #CMA #BusinessStrategy #CostAccounting #NPV #Trend #Analysis #FinancialDecisions #Trendanalysis #USCMA**  
Aishwarya Suthar liked this  
[View Post](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/finance-entrepreneurship-cma-activity-7367846097689444352-WFre)  
![Activity Image](https://media.licdn.com/dms/image/v2/D5622AQGwZHXKcAexZw/feedshare-shrink_800/B56Zj_Y.YYG4Ag-/0/1756631394831?e=2147483647&v=beta&t=TBR_APg5AAh-LEkyeLfBgy8wP_Rw4M-Xi077ZCQ13Zc)

- **IFRS has become the global language of financial reporting, adopted in more than 140 countries to bring consistency, transparency, and comparability to financial statements. Yet, history shows us that even with these standards, companies have found ways to mislead, from Satyam’s inflated revenues to Enron’s off-balance-sheet entities, Carillion’s goodwill valuations, and the 2008 financial crisis driven by financial instruments. While summing up my SBR syllabus as exams are approaching, I’ve realised that IFRSs are not just academic content, they are powerful tools that shape how businesses communicate their financial reality. Among the wide range of standards, some stand out as truly game-changing: 🔹 IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts with Customers Revenue drives valuations and investor sentiment. Small tweaks in contract terms or timing of recognition can drastically shift reported earnings. It’s no surprise that “creative revenue recognition” is a common tool in corporate fraud. 🔹 IFRS 16 – Leases Before IFRS 16, billions in lease liabilities were kept off-balance sheet. By bringing these onto the financial statements, this standard reshaped balance sheets across industries, particularly airlines, retail, and logistics. 🔹 IFRS 9 – Financial Instruments Banks and corporates alike rely heavily on financial instruments. The expected credit loss (ECL) model requires judgement, and that judgement can sometimes be stretched, creating gaps between the true risk and reported figures. 🔹 IAS 38 & Group Consolidation – Intangibles & Impairments Internally generated brands are not recognised in individual financial statements. However, brands and other identifiable intangibles acquired in a business combination are recognised at consolidation (separately from goodwill), making valuation and impairment key topics. 🔹 Conceptual Framework v/s IFRSs An interesting observation: while the IASB removed “probability” from the definitions of assets and liabilities in the revised Conceptual Framework, probability thresholds still appear within several IFRSs (e.g., IAS 37’s “probable outflow”; IFRS 15’s “highly probable” constraint on variable consideration/collectability). This shows how standard-setting is a continuous process, not a one-time rulebook. 💡 What stands out to me is that while these standards aim for transparency and comparability, companies often operate in the grey areas and at times exploit them, leading to distortions and even large-scale frauds. ✨ During my preparation, I’ve come to realise that mastering these standards goes far beyond passing an exam, it’s about developing the ability to look beyond the numbers, apply true professional scepticism, and ensure financial reporting reflects business reality with integrity. 👉 What do you think, which IFRS has the biggest influence on shaping corporate reporting today? #IFRS #ACCA #SBR #FinancialReporting #AccountingStandards #FinanceProfessionals #EthicsInFinance #Transparency**  
Aishwarya Suthar liked this  
[View Post](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/vikrampaliwalll_ifrs-acca-sbr-activity-7366114326518161410-nsnD)

![](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/3d/9d/8742980343fab372b3b118919c3b/financialplanning-brand-logo-color-no-padding.svg)

# Wealth management M&A hits high in 2024, 2025 growth expected

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | [About Frank](https://www.financial-planning.com/author/frank-gargano) |
| [mailto](mailto: frank.gargano@arizent.com) | [frank.gargano@arizent.com](mailto: frank.gargano@arizent.com) |
| [linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fgargano) | [fgargano](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fgargano) |

![Complimentary Access Pill](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/f6/04/5082fa344b4ca9e82d1e28b83baa/complimentary-access-pill-green.svg)

2024 was a productive year for merger-and-acquisition activity across the wealth management landscape, featuring notable deals such as Wealth Enhancement Group's purchase of Levy Wealth Management Group and Hightower's majority stake in the Boston-based NEPC. Experts say appetites for such deals are poised to grow in 2025.

[Hightower](https://www.financial-planning.com/news/hightower-deal-amasses-1-8t-in-assets), a large advisory firm in Chicago, announced its purchase of a majority ownership share of NEPC in October. NEPC, which is an investment consultant and private wealth advisor, oversees more than $1.6 trillion in client assets. Together, the firms will have roughly $1.8 trillion in assets under administration and $258 billion in assets under management.

[Wealth Enhancement Group](https://www.financial-planning.com/news/wealth-enhancements-latest-acquisition-puts-it-over-90b-in-aum), putting its private equity backing to work, will absorb Levy Wealth's $1.3 billion in assets under management for an undisclosed amount, pushing its total AUM to more than $90 billion. The organization inked five deals in the first half of 2024 that yielded a combined $2.4 billion in assets, according to data from consulting firm and investment bank [Echelon Partners](https://7475083.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/7475083/ECHELON%20Q2%202024%20RIA%20M&A%20Deal%20Report_Media%20Release.pdf).

Jim Cahn, chair of the investment committee and chief strategy officer at Wealth Enhancement, said that deal volume across the industry will continue to grow as scale continues to drive "better client outcomes and more efficiency within the RIA space."

"We see a lot of new entrants trying to gain scale and bidding for deals, but it will be hard to catch up to the current industry leaders as they have an advantage," Cahn said. "Sellers are better off working with an experienced buyer."

**READ MORE:** [**6 ways independent wealth management firms are consolidating**](https://www.financial-planning.com/list/how-wealth-management-firms-are-consolidating-in-2024)

Despite the private equity wave sweeping across the wealth management landscape, as firms sell ownership stakes to outsiders in exchange for a stable source of funding for growth, many leaders are remaining independent or pursuing mergers with competitors to achieve scale while retaining full control.

Leaders of the Atlanta-based SFA Partners, which has roughly $7 billion in AUM, said in a prior interview with Financial Planning that while aware of the benefits of private equity financing, the tradeoff of ownership struggles isn't worth it.

"We just celebrated 21 years in the business. … So in 21 years, you can obviously tell that firms that started around the same time that we did, a lot of them are gone," Jamie Mackay, president and chief operating officer of SFA, said in an interview with FP's [Dan Shaw](https://www.financial-planning.com/author/dan-shaw). "A lot of them are gone because they've been packaged up by private equity and eventually sold."

**READ MORE:** [**Why some RIAs aren't riding the private equity wave**](https://www.financial-planning.com/news/why-some-rias-reject-private-equity-for-wealth-consolidation)

All of this is subject to change. As President-elect Donald Trump's administration begins its work amid a lowered interest rate environment, regulatory experts are predicting deal volume to accelerate.

"M&A looks to accelerate in 2025 as the Trump administration takes over and brings changes in leadership at the Department of Justice and Federal Trade Commission. … The DOJ's and FTC's current leadership has been pro-consumer and seen as a stalwart against corporate consolidation," said Joseph J. Raetzer, business lawyer and consultant.

Raul Gastesi, partner and cofounder of the Florida-based law firm Gastesi, Lopez & Mestre, agreed that "less regulation and a change in leadership at the FTC" will have a significant effect on M&A activity.

Read on to learn more about the high-profile deals announced in 2024.

![Cetera headquarters](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3c0620b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/720x452+0+0/resize/740x465!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fe7%2Fb0%2F5a20d7f94589b18fa7eb3cc972f8%2F200-n-sepulveda-2-twitter.jpg)

## Cetera Financial Group acquires Concourse Financial Group Securities

The San Diego-based Cetera Financial Group [announced on Oct. 8](https://cetera.com/press-room/cetera-announces-agreement-with-protective-life-to-acquire-concourse-financial-group-securities) that it plans to acquire Concourse Financial Group Securities through an asset acquisition deal.

The deal for CFGS, a Birmingham, Alabama-based subsidiary of insurance company Protective Life Corporation, is anticipated to net Cetera roughly 350 advisors with $4 billion in assets under management and $12 billion under administration. Cetera, with 12,000 finance experts and $224 billion under management, expects to finalize the acquisition in the first quarter of this year for an untold amount.

"Cetera has a proven track record of acquiring and successfully integrating independent broker-dealers affiliated with insurance organizations, and Concourse Financial Group Securities represents a tremendous opportunity in today's rapidly consolidating market," Mike Durbin, CEO of Cetera Holdings, said in a [press release](https://cetera.com/press-room/cetera-announces-agreement-with-protective-life-to-acquire-concourse-financial-group-securities).

**READ MORE:** [**Cetera picks up insurer's wealth unit with Concourse Financial purchase**](https://www.financial-planning.com/news/cetera-scooping-up-4b-aum-concourse-financial)

![Wealth Enhancement Group Building in Minneapolis, Minnesota](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/a359d1a/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5502x3668+0+0/resize/740x493!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2F3e%2F25%2F24209c1e49e484958f14b10693c7%2Fadobestock-391886637-editorial-use-only.jpeg)

## The breakup between LPL and WEG

Last month, leaders with the Minneapolis-based investment advisory firm Wealth Enhancement Group confirmed predictions that it was the second of two large entities to break away from LPL Financial following an announcement during a [July earnings call](https://www.financial-planning.com/news/lpls-earnings-fall-in-q2-as-two-osjs-leave-firm).

WEG partnered with LPL for more than 17 years as an office of supervisory jurisdiction, which is a sizable practice that works within an independent broker-dealer to execute specific supervisory functions. [Merit Financial Advisors](https://docs.google.com/document/u/0/d/1jEIs6A0Vr8Bw77jXnTlZnXLAs_-qhpSRZIiEsqZalcc/edit), a hybrid firm with about $12 billion under management, was the first to leave LPL and departed in August of last year.

"The two firms will be amicably ending their relationship effective June 30, 2025, as each pursues its own growth objectives," according to a WEG spokesperson.

**READ MORE:** [**LPL, WEG part ways in second large OSJ departure**](https://www.financial-planning.com/news/lpl-separating-from-wealth-enhancement-group)

![BlackRock](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/b852ddf/2147483647/strip/true/crop/500x333+0+0/resize/740x493!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2F59%2F46%2Fcb61bd0243f4aae2c310c4367b60%2Fblackrock.jpg)

## BlackRock's $12 billion purchase of HPS

Executives at BlackRock struck a deal to purchase the New York-based HPS Investment Partners last month in an all-stock agreement valued at roughly $12 billion.

Scott Kapnick, Scot French and Michael Patterson, founders of HPS, will go on to lead a private financing business unit within BlackRock upon finalization of the deal, seeing the trio join BlackRock's global executive committee and Kapnick additionally become an observer to the firm's board of directors. The new unit will "unite direct lending, fund finance, and BlackRock's GP and LP solutions (fund of funds, GP/LP secondaries, co-investments)," according to a [press release](https://ir.blackrock.com/news-and-events/press-releases/press-releases-details/2024/BlackRock-to-Acquire-HPS-Investment-Partners-to-Deliver-Integrated-Solutions-Across-Public-and-Private-Markets/default.aspx).

The combination of the two will position BlackRock with about $220 billion in assets and room to grow with insurance clients and in investment-grade private debt, the firm said.

**READ MORE:** [**BlackRock buys private credit firm HPS in $12B deal**](https://www.financial-planning.com/articles/blackrock-buys-private-credit-firm-hps-in-12b-deal)

![The Robinhood application is displayed in the App Store on an Apple Inc. iPhone in an arranged photograph taken in Washington, D.C., U.S., on Friday, Dec. 14, 2018. The Securities Investor Protection Corp. said a new checking account from Robinhood Financial LLC raises red flags and that the deposited funds may not be eligible for protection. Photographer: Andrew Harrer/Bloomberg](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/17a2709/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5000x3333+0+0/resize/740x493!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fd4%2Fe7%2Ff6dd89b4436d8f54f3c5d7b29abf%2Frobinhood-application-is-displayed-in-the-app-store-on-an-apple-inc.%20iPhone%20in%20an%20arranged%20photograph%20taken%20in%20Washington%2C%20D.C.%2C%20Dec.%2014%2C%202018.jpg)

## Robinhood announces $300 million deal to buy TradePMR

Robinhood Markets, the California financial services company, announced that it plans to purchase the custodial and portfolio management platform provider TradePMR in a cash and stock deal worth roughly $300 million.

The purchase, which current predictions expect to close sometime in the first half of this year, will provide Robinhood with a wealth of registered investment advisors for its current client base and further its path into the retail investment markets, according to the [press release](https://newsroom.aboutrobinhood.com/robinhood-to-acquire-tradepmr/). TradePMR currently has more than $40 billion in assets under management.

"The TradePMR team has one of the strongest RIA networks in the industry," Vlad Tenev, chairman and CEO at Robinhood, said in the release. "We're excited to join forces to build a category-defining advisory platform for the next generation."

**READ MORE:** [**Robinhood to buy TradePMR for $300 million in wealth client push**](https://www.financial-planning.com/articles/robinhood-to-buy-tradepmr-in-push-for-wealthy-clients)

![M&A - Connected puzzle pieces with the acronym M&A. Mergers and acquisitions dealmaking deals merger acquisition](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/7a15dec/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5676x3784+0+0/resize/740x493!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2F01%2F34%2Fc4dbab1240dc8578c3c771711ad1%2Fadobestock-434051383.jpeg)

## Pathstone's groundbreaking purchase of Hall Capital Partners

[Pathstone](https://www.pathstone.com/team/), the Englewood, New Jersey-based fee-only registered investment advisory firm, announced it had inked a deal to purchase [Hall Capital Partners](https://hallcapital.com/) for an undisclosed amount.

If finalized, the acquisition of Hall Capital would boost Pathstone's total assets under management to $100 billion and total assets under advisement and administration to roughly $160 billion — making it the firm's largest purchase ever. The New York- and San Francisco-based Hall Capital oversees 130 clients with roughly $45 billion in assets.

"From the beginning, we have strived and prided ourselves on our ability to meet the needs of our clients, and we truly believe this combination brings together two complementary organizations who will benefit immensely from collaboration and sharing of resources," Katie Hall, cofounder and cochair of Hall Capital, said in an [October 2024 press release](https://www.pathstone.com/press/pathstone-and-hall-capital-to-combine-forces/).

**Read more:** [**Pathstone to acquire $45B firm, reach almost $160B in client assets**](https://www.financial-planning.com/news/pathstone-to-acquire-hall-capital-partners-in-largest-deal)

![](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0dbbc5e/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4677x4677+75+0/resize/48x48!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2F53%2F76%2F411bee964df0adac9a6f304ed775%2Farizant-5-11-223489.jpg)

Schwab services 16,000 RIAs with 2,000 different fee structures. According to the industry's largest custodian, the exact costs come down to "a very personalized negotiation" with the firms.

![fp_author_Tobias_Salinger.jpg](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/e2ccafb/2147483647/strip/true/crop/600x600+0+0/resize/48x48!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2F58%2Fb1%2F0d860f6d4a10a4b7965b6370eb99%2Ffp-author-tobias-salinger.jpg)

For our Know Your Niche series, Steve Mason, with Bank of America's Private Bank, has developed a client base out of horse lovers like himself.

![](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/4424986/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3430x3430+0+0/resize/48x48!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fa1%2F73%2F34c4e41748aeb9b64a25285017d4%2Fbrian-wallheimer-2.jpg)

After experiencing $14 billion in assets outflows in the last quarter of 2025, UBS brought in more than $5 billion in the first quarter of this year.

![Dan Shaw](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f7cbf19/2147483647/strip/true/crop/486x486+14+0/resize/48x48!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fae%2F9a%2Fc6dace0f400f87fc6f30bb33f9f9%2Fdanshaw-2.jpg)

The newly formed Cetera Planning Partners is part of the firm's plan to broaden its channel for advisors working as direct employees.

![](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/297496e/2147483647/strip/true/crop/1920x1080+0+0/resize/1280x720!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2F6d%2Fd5%2Fc90dda1d472e8d87343b20fdf614%2Fhanau-cetera.png)

A federal judge finds that the embattled brokerage Alpine Securities' argument that FINRA should answer to the federal executive branch amounts to " wishful thinking" that "collapses under the weight of spiraling aspiration."

![Dan Shaw](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f7cbf19/2147483647/strip/true/crop/486x486+14+0/resize/48x48!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fae%2F9a%2Fc6dace0f400f87fc6f30bb33f9f9%2Fdanshaw-2.jpg)
![Sign outside offic eof Financial Industry Regulatory Authority ](https://arizent.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/c5c0ef3/2147483647/strip/true/crop/500x281+0+44/resize/1280x720!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fsource-media-brightspot.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fac%2Ffd%2F400304fa407697b3741a497301df%2Ffinra-retail-shot-taken-by-michael-chu.jpg)

The firms' collaboration shines a light on how the wealth management business works today and how it is evolving, as advisors weigh independence against the risks of "poking the bear" when they leave.

SOURCE: https://www.sdmmag.com/articles/105123-a-year-of-surprises-m-and-a-activity-in-2025-slightly-improves
Title: A Year of Surprises: M&A Activity in 2025 Slightly Improves
# A Year of Surprises: M&A Activity in 2025 Slightly Improves

## In the world of security mergers and acquisitions (M&A), 2025 delivered a surprising year, from a large spark of confidence in the residential sector to the lack of financial impact despite high interest rates and tariffs.

![Consolidation trends](https://www.sdmmag.com/ext/resources/Issues/2026/02-February/SDM-0226-F3-Feat-Slide1-1170x658.webp?t=1772048449)

Consolidation trends were on the rise in 2025, as companies in fire and life safety looked to expand their expertise and services, and as innovative companies aimed to differentiate their tech stacks.

Like the year prior, the fire and life safety space saw a steady number of mergers and acquisitions (M&A) activity in 2025. Pye-Barker Fire & Safety continued to dominate in this regard. For another consecutive year, Pye-Barker completed, likely, the most acquisitions in the industry, with over 30 under its belt in 2025. The company now has a presence in 47 U.S. states, having newly entered Arkansas and Hawaii.

RapidFire Safety & Security, which offers expertise in fire, life safety and physical security services to the commercial sector, also completed multiple acquisitions, further adding to M&A activity in fire and life safety. Other notable acquisitions in the security dealer and integrator space include Alarm New England’s acquisition of Associated Alarm Systems, American Alarm’s acquisition of Instant Signal & Alarm, ORR Protection’s acquisition of Compass Fire Protection, Sciens’ acquisition of Fire Security & Sound Systems in N.Y. and Security Fire Systems’ acquisition of Lakeview Security, Fire & Communications.

That’s not to say 2025 was [a full repeat of 2024](/articles/103856-2025-outlook-for-m-and-a-in-security-excellent) in terms of M&A activity. The year started off strong with the acquisition of Sonitrol New Orleans by Securitas Technology, bringing together unique security capabilities and expansive technology resources. This combination was a sign of coming trends and targets: technology, unification and consolidation.

### The Major Trends

According to Raymond James’ 2025 Security & Safety Year-End Report, M&A activity began to strengthen in the third quarter, following a volatile start to the year. “Deal flow regained momentum as market participants have become more comfortable underwriting larger and more complex transactions, particularly in sectors demonstrating resilience and operational strength,” the report said. “The rebound reflects a broader shift toward optimism as market participants position for growth heading into year-end.”

*SDM* reported on nearly 100 acquisitions in 2025. Approximately 65% were completed in the second half of the year. Overall, 2025 was a decent year for M&A activity, and a slight improvement from 2024. Still, it didn’t measure up to the pre-COVID environment.

“You can’t overlook what occurred in 2020 with the pandemic,” says Les E. Gold, partner, Mitchell Silberberg & Knupp LLP, Los Angeles, Calif., and contributing [legal columnist](/topics/2251-security-the-law) for *SDM*. “That slowed things up. I think this is still the aftermath where people are waking up a little bit, and they’re looking for growth. But I think the most important thing is the tremendous amount of money that’s available in the private equity industry. They have money. They have to pay their investors, so they have to get the money invested. I think that’s had a lot to do with the increase in activity in the M&A area in 2025.”

Kelly Bond, partner and co-founder, Bridgepoint Advisors, Frisco, Texas, adds, “The M&A environment in 2025 was shaped largely by a small group of highly active buyers that completed several premium, well-publicized transactions. The visibility of these high-priced deals encouraged more business owners to explore the market, motivated by the possibility of achieving similarly strong outcomes.”

Looking for quick answers on security topics?
**Try Ask SDM, our new smart AI search tool.**
[Ask SDM →](https://www.sdmmag.com/ask-sdm?pk=article_ask_SDM)

The start of the year was impacted by financial concerns, which contributed to shaping the landscape throughout 2025. “Interest rates remained on the high side at the beginning of the year but then started to trend downward toward the end of the year; it’s yet to be determined what impact this will have on M&A activity,” say the collective experts at Barnes Associates, Orlando, Fla. “The industry continues to have strong support from specific lenders but could always use more debt providers. Private equity continues to be interested but is looking for the best performers in high growth sectors, especially where AI and video are being leveraged.”

Bond agrees. “For businesses with the right DNA, valuations remained highly competitive,” she says. “One of the most notable factors was the strength of the cash component; many transactions were completed with 100% cash at closing. In addition, several sellers were given the opportunity to roll equity into the transaction, creating an attractive path to participate in future upside while achieving liquidity.”

Tariffs were also a concern at the beginning of the year, but experts saw little to no impact on the security industry because of them. In fact, Gold says tariffs could have a positive impact on security overall. “If you were to ask me what I think in the future, I don’t think it’s going to impact the security industry because it’s going to impact other industries,” he says. “When it impacts other industries, crime increases. … It could have a very positive impact on the security industry.”

The M&A environment in 2025 was shaped largely by a small group of highly active buyers that completed several premium, well-publicized transactions. The visibility of these high-priced deals encouraged more business owners to explore the market, motivated by the possibility of achieving similarly strong outcomes.

### Technology Influences All

![Mergers and Acquisitions](/ext/resources/Issues/2026/02-February/SDM-0226-F3-Side1-Slide2-1170x658.jpg)

Rapidly evolving technology is playing a major role in every aspect of security. Of course, that means M&A activity has been, and will continue to be, heavily influenced by artificial intelligence, the cloud, the hardware to software shift, and more.

Raymond James’ 2025 M&A Report highlights the shift from hardware to cloud and AI as a major trend in M&A, as both strategic and financial sponsors focused on acquiring software-as-a-service and AI capabilities. In Q1/25, Rhombus acquired Dashdive, a SaaS platform specializing in monitoring and analyzing multi-tenant cloud service costs, to optimize cloud cost structure at scale. Motorola acquired multiple cloud and AI companies, i.e. Theatro Labs, RapidDeploy, InVisit and Blue Eye, to continue expanding and differentiating its technology stack.

“The target profile is definitely shifting with the movement away from pure hardware (OEM) towards the companies with a focus on cloud/SaaS, AI and analytics, a focus on cybersecurity, scale for geographic expansion and/or companies with end-to-end solutions,” says Kim Loy, an independent consultant. “My expectation is we will see the profile shift and the activity accelerate in 2026.”

Rory Russell of Acquisition & Funding Services shares a similar perspective: “Sellers need to understand and embrace advanced technologies like AI and the cloud to make their firms more attractive. ... Numerous specialty consultants have emerged to assist buyers and help evaluate the seller’s internal processes and use of AI.”

Russell adds that larger, established consolidators are sensitive to non-traditional factors, such as technology posture, cybersecurity readiness and adaptation to AI, because it makes integration easier. “Thus, they are willing to pay more for firms who have these capabilities,” he says.

Buyers are increasingly prioritizing companies with scalable software platforms, recurring services and cloud-based infrastructure because these attributes support predictable revenue and operational efficiency, according to Kelly Bond of Bridgepoint Advisors. “Additionally, the continued convergence of cyber and physical security, combined with the growing use of AI for analytics, automation and threat detection, has become a meaningful differentiator, directly impacting both valuations and buyer interest,” she says.

Barnes Associates experts add, “Evolving technologies will continue to be an emphasis, but integration of its components will be key so that dealers can demonstrate the technologies’ capabilities to their customers.”

Les E. Gold of MSK says AI is the top technological factor poised to influence M&A activity. “How is that going to impact the industry? I don’t think anyone really knows, except the big companies are investing very, very heavily in AI,” he says. “I think that some people are going to get hurt in this industry because they have not stayed up with technology. ... I think new technology, new companies coming into the industry are going to affect it dramatically over the next few years. And I think that the companies that stay in it, their valuations are going to increase significantly.”

AI and other technologies are already adding changes to due diligence. “When you’re doing your due diligence now, you have to really look into what the company is doing, and, more importantly, what the company is not doing,” Gold says.

Independent of who the buyer is, they will check to see how AI is going to affect the potential target. Additionally, privacy has become a major issue. “It never has been in the past, but these security companies gain a great deal of information from their customers and subscribers. There’s been litigation regarding privacy issues,” Gold says. “When you’re doing your due diligence, you have to make sure that the company has really stayed up to date and has, in fact, made sure that they protect the confidential information they have. Buyers look at that because that could affect liability when they take these companies over.”

### A Year of Surprises

In 2025, the residential sector made headlines when [GTCR acquired SimpliSafe](/articles/104858-simplisafe-closes-sale-to-gtcr-appoints-ceo-and-president).

“GTCR not only purchased the commercial business of ADT, but they also acquired SimpliSafe, which is strictly a residential company. Effectively, they got out of the residential business, and then they got back in,” Gold says. He adds that Apollo, the private equity group that still owns a residential portion of ADT, now has a new, large competitor with GTCR on the residential side. “There’s a lot to be desired, I think, in what transpired there,” he adds.

“GTCR’s purchase of SimpliSafe was clearly a vote of confidence for the residential security market,” experts from Barnes Associates say. “It will be interesting to see how GTCR’s strategy evolves given their recent emphasis on the commercial sector. Are they moving from specialist to generalist?”

The move demonstrates an unexpected confidence in the residential sector, which fell beneath the M&A radar in 2024 as Pye-Barker put fire and life safety back on the map.

The amount of interest garnered from private equity also served as a surprise for M&A experts, like Rory Russell, owner, Acquisition & Funding Services, Kattskill Bay, N.Y. “Many more firms were actively seeking private companies to acquire, both PE firms as well as large corporations,” he says. “The financial sponsors (PE firms, family offices) have plenty of available cash on hand to do deals.”

Bond adds, “I was genuinely surprised by who ultimately decided to sell. Several business owners came to market whom I would not have expected to be sellers at this stage of their lives or careers, underscoring how compelling market conditions and strategic timing can influence even long-term operators.”

Predominantly, these private equity groups, as well as some strategic buyers, targeted companies that combine hardware with software/cloud/RMR. “The focus seemed to be on platform builds and carve-outs,” says Kim Loy, an independent consultant and deal advisor. “I believe that there were some hesitancies in the market (mid- to late-2025) due to economic uncertainty and what the overall impact of the tariffs would ultimately lead to. It may or may not have been a big surprise to many; however, the strength of valuations of AI/cloud-enabled players was at a premium with a strong push to monetize analytics and related services.”

GTCR not only purchased the commercial business of ADT, but they also acquired SimpliSafe, which is strictly a residential company. Effectively, they got out of the residential business, and then they got back in.

### What Are Buyers Looking For?

Buyers are placing greater emphasis on transparency, consistency and defensible financial reporting.

“Buyers, lenders and investors evaluate businesses based on factors that reflect management discipline and operational performance, and that becomes apparent quickly when reporting is inconsistent or incomplete,” says Amy Becker, president, Astute Financial Consulting, Fort Collins, Colo. “When companies are unable to clearly track or explain these metrics, diligence often slows, and perceived risk increases. A clean RMR roll-forward showing beginning RMR, additions, cancellations and ending RMR is especially important because it helps buyers understand both the quality and sustainability of recurring revenue.”

Experts from Barnes Associate highlight the well-capitalized group of buyers in the marketplace that are trying to build out national platforms. “There is also a portion of the pool of owners at the independent and regional level that have reached the age where they are deciding what the future holds for them,” the group says. “The confluence of season of life with exit potential is tipping the scales for many owners.”

Loy believes that M&A will favor companies that have the monetization of services figured out and anticipates companies with scalable, privacy-preserving analytics will be in demand. Products that support mobile/biometrics and ACaaS models are also likely to see increased M&A interest from buyers. “Many offerings in this sector are still viewed as legacy brands, and many end users with enterprise systems are still migrating. Early adopters may be undervalued,” she says.

The M&A landscape is in a slightly tougher spot thanks to technological trends, as buyers are becoming more “picky” with their targets. “Acquirers are increasingly targeting AI‑first platforms, identity management tools and cloud‑native hosting providers, signaling a broader shift toward a more software‑driven security landscape,” says Steve Rubin, partner and co-founder, Bridgepoint Advisors. “That momentum is set to intensify in 2026. Expect faster dealmaking, deeper moves into IoT and OT security and continued expansion of managed services. Major players are preparing for larger, more strategic acquisitions, while smaller firms may struggle unless they offer truly differentiated AI or IoT capabilities.”

Jason Grelle, partner and co-founder, Bridgepoint Advisors, highlights a few areas that buyers are going to staunchly avoid: legacy systems, customer and revenue concentration risk, third-party vulnerabilities and weak supply-chain security. “Recurring revenue economics are under pressure in the alarm/security space,” he says. “Specifically, rising *cost to create recurring revenue* (RMR) and inflationary pressures can erode financial attractiveness,” he adds. “Expect buyers to scrutinize loyalty metrics, churn trends and cost of RMR creation more rigorously.”

Expect faster dealmaking, deeper moves into IoT and OT security and continued expansion of managed services. Major players are preparing for larger, more strategic acquisitions, while smaller firms may struggle unless they offer truly differentiated AI or IoT capabilities.

### Article Contributions, Services & Advice

The following companies contributed to this article and offer services, as well as additional advice, for security companies that may be interested in selling in 2026.

![Acquisition & Funding Services (AFS)](/ext/resources/Issues/2026/02-February/SDM-0226-F3-Side2-AFS-Slide3-1170x658.jpg)

#### Acquisition & Funding Services

[Acquisition & Funding Services (AFS)](https://www.afssmartfunding.com/) is an alarm company broker specializing in fire, CCTV, integration and security alarm business acquisitions, sales, financing and mergers. A trusted and experienced alarm company broker, AFS provides a variety of services including business valuations, alarm company financing programs, guidance through the selling process and more to security businesses large and small.

**Piece of Advice:** “Do your research. Not all buyers are the same. Look closely at deal terms, not just the sell price. Find an advisor like AFS with deep industry experience to help evaluate in-bound inquiries and deal with interested buyers.” — *Rory Russell*

![Astute Financial Consulting](/ext/resources/Issues/2026/02-February/SDM-0226-F3-Side2-Astute-Slide4-1170x658.jpg)

#### Astute Financial Consulting

[Astute Financial Consulting](https://www.astutefinancial.biz/) helps security and life safety companies operate with financial clarity and confidence. The company focuses on helping companies build strong financial practices such as clear RMR tracking, disciplined reporting and accurate accrual-based financial statements. This preparation allows companies to respond effectively to questions from buyers, lenders or investors.

**Piece of Advice:** “Whether you are thinking about selling your business or not, you should operate as if you are. Potential buyers, lenders and investors value a business based on factors that stem from how it is managed and how consistently it performs. The discipline required to prepare for a pote

2025 Trends Report

## Annual Mergers & Acquisitions Outlook Survey

For 21 years, Dykema’s Annual M&A Outlook Survey has tracked the pulse of dealmaking in the U.S., consistently affirming a central truth: the M&A market reflects the health of the broader economy. From the lows of 2008 to the post-COVID rebound, and through cycles of regulatory shifts, election-year volatility, and interest rate fluctuations, dealmakers have adapted to changing conditions. This year’s survey—fielded in August and September 2025—gathers insights from 216 professionals, including executives, attorneys, bankers, and private equity leaders, offering a comprehensive view of the current landscape.

Despite ongoing economic uncertainty, respondents express cautious optimism. Strategic acquisitions, AI integration, and operational efficiency have emerged as top priorities. Private equity is expected to drive deal activity, with increased emphasis on due diligence and ESG risk screening. AI is no longer a future consideration—it’s actively reshaping how deals are sourced, evaluated, and executed. While challenges remain, including tariffs and valuation gaps, the data suggests that dealmakers are recalibrating rather than retreating. The M&A market continues to evolve, and its resilience remains a defining feature.

[Read the full Executive Summary](https://www.dykema.com/MnA-2025/executive-summary.html)

![Image 2](https://www.dykema.com/a/web/rTo7RAJGQR8Ngt3wKWCwrb/aSY1YD/frost_andrew-web-ii.jpg)

As we look ahead to 2026, our clients are cautiously optimistic. The fundamentals—dry powder, strategic demand, and sector-specific momentum—are in place, but dealmaking will hinge on clarity around interest rates, regulatory shifts, and geopolitical stability. We expect more creative structuring, tighter diligence, and a renewed focus on risk allocation as buyers and sellers adapt to a more dynamic environment.

Andrew Frost, Member, Milwaukee

![Image 3](https://www.dykema.com/a/web/9JKEyatHrcXaHfAWTpVCP9/aSXZZz/bechen_kate-web-ii.jpg)

In today’s capital markets, M&A deals aren’t stalling—they’re evolving. Attorneys are no longer just reacting to volatility; we’re engineering deal structures and features that anticipate it. The capital markets may be turbulent, but the legal strategy behind transactions is more agile and data-driven than ever.

Kate Bechen, Member, Milwaukee

![Image 4](https://www.dykema.com/a/web/vXWiNkbUPLypwZPoK9wtR9/a611Xt/lutringer_kari-web.jpg)

Texas continues to be a hotbed for M&A activity, but the tone has shifted. We’re seeing clients become more deliberate—scrutinizing valuations, adjusting deal structures, and leaning heavily on due diligence to navigate market volatility. In sectors like energy and infrastructure, strategic consolidation is driving deal flow, with buyers focused on expanding capabilities and positioning for long-term competitiveness.

Kari Lutringer, Member, Houston

![Image 5](https://www.dykema.com/a/web/kMXtRTW4C9ZFX6TxfDiFMK/8XonFJ/buckley_sean_2024_bio_dallas.jpg)

Technology transactions are increasingly shaped by the race for innovation. Deals are being driven by the need to secure proprietary platforms, AI capabilities, and scalable data infrastructure. Legal teams are navigating complex issues around IP ownership, algorithmic accountability, and cross-border data compliance—making sector expertise essential to structuring deals that protect core assets while enabling integration.

Sean M. Buckley, Member, Dallas

![Image 6](https://www.dykema.com/a/web/gbx72TgMi5X5kiNWF9exBM/7J6G4y/waltzpeter-bio.jpg)

Our clients are increasingly recognizing that securities issues aren’t just a legal formality—they’re central to deal success. Whether it’s structuring equity rollovers, navigating disclosure obligations, or ensuring exemption from registration, securities considerations are shaping how deals are negotiated and closed. We’re seeing more clients ask for proactive guidance to avoid regulatory missteps and preserve deal value, especially in transactions involving complex ownership structures or public-facing entities.

Peter Waltz, Member, Milwaukee

![Image 7](https://www.dykema.com/a/web/rTo7RAJGQR8Ngt3wKWCwrb/aSY1YD/frost_andrew-web-ii.jpg)

As we look ahead to 2026, our clients are cautiously optimistic. The fundamentals—dry powder, strategic demand, and sector-specific momentum—are in place, but dealmaking will hinge on clarity around interest rates, regulatory shifts, and geopolitical stability. We expect more creative structuring, tighter diligence, and a renewed focus on risk allocation as buyers and sellers adapt to a more dynamic environment.

Sectors Most Likely To See M&A Activity In The Next 12 Months

This year’s results note some notable shifts in sector expectations compared to previous years. The 2025 survey results show a dramatic reshuffling of anticipated M&A activity. Technology, Media, and Telecom jumped from fifth place to first, while Healthcare and Financial Services both lost ground. Notably, Life Sciences and Supply Chain debuted in the top five, pushing out Energy and Consumer Products, which had previously been among the most active sectors.

2024 Ranking 2025
Healthcare**1**Technology, Media, and Telecoms▲ +4
Financial Services**2**Healthcare▼ -1
Energy and Natural Resources**3**Life Sciences▲**_NEW_**
Consumer Products**4**Supply Chain▲**_NEW_**
Technology, Media, and Telecoms**5**Financial Services ▼ -3

## In Retrospect: 20 Years of M&A Outlook

The M&A market has gone for quite a ride since we launched our first outlook report two decades ago.

From the dramatic lows of 2008, to the tech-driven surge of 2015, to the post-pandemic SPAC boom, here’s a look back at the moments that transformed the landscape and impacted dealmaking for years to come.

Methodology

In August and September 2025, national law firm Dykema in partnership with [INSIGHTS2PROFIT](https://www.insight2profit.com/) distributed its M&A Outlook Survey via email and online panel to a group of CEOs, CFOs, and other senior company officers and executives, as well as bankers, private equity professionals, and advisors engaged in M&A. A total of 216 respondents completed the survey, representing more than a dozen sectors, including financial services, technology, media, telecoms, healthcare, industrial/manufacturing, retail, energy and natural resources, real estate and construction, consumer products, education, logistics, automotive, transportation and mobility, food and beverage, and life sciences. To allow for crosstabulation analysis, a higher proportion of respondents were engaged in the automotive, transportation and mobility, healthcare, and energy and natural resources sectors. Respondents included public and private c

# MTD Series Recap: Design Choices, Discipline & Complexity

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

[![Image 2: View profile for Chris Downing](https://media.licdn.com/dms/image/v2/D4E03AQEnUVU_je5Feg/profile-displayphoto-scale_400_400/B4EZz60GU8IUAg-/0/1773734489288?e=2147483647&v=beta&t=VlixKkpQQn2rYAWINV7bOVOzF5hNEsCmqPDUqalFwyc)](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/chris-downing?trk=public_post_feed-actor-image)

[Chris Downing](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/chris-downing?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

Sage•14K followers

 2mo 

*   [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fchris-downing_mtd-week-7-activity-7431304554509471744-w84d&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

This will be the last one of this particular style and series. Over the past seven weeks I’ve shared 35 daily posts on Making Tax Digital for Income Tax. Short. Focused. Practical. Hopefully informative. It’s time to change the style slightly from here - but before that, here’s Week 7 pulled together in one place. This week we covered: • Why multiple trades mean multiple updates - MTD operates per business, not per individual. One trade and one rental property could mean eight submissions a year… but still one penalty position. • Why digital records still need originals - software structures the data, but invoices, receipts and bank statements still matter. Digital isn’t a substitute for evidence. • Why the accruals decision now sits in Q1 - cash is default, accruals by election, and once chosen it’s locked for the year. This is no longer just a year-end choice. • Why calendar quarters are optional - election required, software dependent, deadlines unchanged. It’s a workflow decision, not an automatic setting. • And why quarterly errors fix forward - updates are cumulative. Correct next quarter. Fix the digital records. Focus energy on the year-end position If there’s one theme running through all 35 posts, it’s this: MTD isn’t just about submissions. It’s about design choices, workflow discipline and understanding where complexity actually sits. Thank you to everyone who’s followed along, commented or challenged the thinking. Next phase - same topic, slightly different lens. Making Tax Digital isn’t just a software change. It’s a behaviour change. A lot of performers are treating MTD like: “Oh I’ll download something soon and it’ll sort itself out.” It won’t. From April 2026 (if you earn over £50k self-employed income), you’ll need to: • Keep digital records • Submit quarterly updates • File a final year-end declaration That means no more shoeboxes in January. No more “I’ll sort it after panto season.” No more pretending your bank feed is “basically bookkeeping.” MTD rewards the organised. If your income comes from gigs, teaching, royalties, grants, or multiple projects, now is the time to tighten things up. Because the deadline might be in April...but the preparation window is right now. 🚨 MTD is no longer “coming” — it’s happening in April 2026 From 6 April 2026, Making Tax Digital for Income Tax will apply to sole traders and landlords earning over £50,000 — impacting over 864,000 taxpayers immediately. Yet many businesses are still not prepared for what this actually means in practice: ➡️ Quarterly reporting ➡️ Digital record-keeping ➡️ HMRC-compatible software This is where most fall behind — not awareness, but execution. So what should you be doing now? We’ve put together a clear, practical MTD checklist covering: ✔️ Whether you fall within scope ✔️ Getting set up with the right software ✔️ Moving from spreadsheets to compliant digital records ✔️ Understanding quarterly submissions ✔️ Avoiding common (and costly) mistakes Because once MTD starts, there’s no transition period luxury — compliance becomes part of your day-to-day operations. 📊 And this is just phase one… millions more will be brought in shortly after. 👉 If you want to stay ahead (and avoid unnecessary stress), start here: 👉🏼🔗 [https://lnkd.in/efcn_wPK](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flnkd%2Ein%2Fefcn_wPK&urlhash=zSIm&trk=public_post-text) 👈🏼 [#MTD](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmtd&trk=public_post-text)[#MakingTaxDigital](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmakingtaxdigital&trk=public_post-text)[#UKTax](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fuktax&trk=public_post-text)[#SmallBusinessUK](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fsmallbusinessuk&trk=public_post-text)[#TaxCompliance](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ftaxcompliance&trk=public_post-text)[#Finance](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Ffinance&trk=public_post-text)[#Accountants](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Faccountants&trk=public_post-text)[#SelfAssessment](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fselfassessment&trk=public_post-text)

# Humans only

Glassdoor has been built on the contributions of real employees and job seekers. We use advanced security systems to keep our site safe and prevent misuse or unauthorized access.

In rare cases, security protections may mistakenly block legitimate activity from real users. If you have been mistakenly blocked from accessing our site, please review our [Help Center article](https://help.glassdoor.com/s/article/Troubleshooting-Tips?language=en_US) for troubleshooting steps.

# Réservé aux humains

Glassdoor est fondé sur les contributions de véritables employés et chercheurs d’emploi. Nous utilisons des systèmes de sécurité sophistiqués pour assurer la sécurité de notre site et empêcher toute utilisation abusive ou tout accès non autorisé.

Dans de rares cas, ces protections de sécurité peuvent bloquer par erreur les activités légitimes d’utilisateurs réels. Si votre accès à notre site a été bloqué par erreur, veuillez consulter l’[article de notre centre d’aide](https://aide.glassdoor.fr/s/article/Astuces-de-depannage?language=fr) pour prendre connaissance des étapes de dépannage.

# Nur für Menschen

Glassdoor wurde auf den Beiträgen echter Mitarbeiter:innen und Stellensuchenden aufgebaut. Wir verwenden fortschrittliche Sicherheitssysteme, um unsere Seite sicher zu halten und Missbrauch oder unbefugten Zugriff zu verhindern.

In seltenen Fällen können Sicherheitsvorkehrungen irrtümlich legitime Aktivitäten von echten Nutzerinnen und Nutzern blockieren. Falls Ihr Zugriff auf unsere Seite versehentlich gesperrt wurde, lesen Sie bitte unseren [Artikel im Hilfebereich](https://hilfe.glassdoor.de/s/article/Tipps-zur-Fehlerbehebung?language=de) mit Schritten zur Fehlerbehebung.

# Alleen voor mensen

Glassdoor vertrouwt op de bijdragen van echte werknemers en werkzoekenden. We gebruiken geavanceerde beveiligingssystemen om onze site veilig te houden en misbruik of ongeautoriseerde toegang te voorkomen.

In zeldzame gevallen kunnen beveiligingsmaatregelen onbedoeld legitieme activiteiten van echte gebruikers blokkeren. Als je onverwacht geen toegang krijgt tot onze site, bekijk dan ons [Helpcenter-artikel](https://help.glassdoor.nl/s/article/Tips-voor-het-oplossen-van-problemen?language=nl_NL) voor stappen om het probleem op te lossen.

# Solo personas reales

Glassdoor se basa en las contribuciones de verdaderos empleados y personas que buscan empleo. Usamos sistemas de seguridad avanzados para mantener la seguridad del sitio web y evitar todo uso indebido o el acceso no autorizado.

En casos excepcionales, las medidas de seguridad pueden bloquear por error la actividad de usuarios reales. Si por error no puedes acceder a nuestro sitio web, consulta el [artículo en el Centro de ayuda](https://ayuda.glassdoor.com.mx/s/article/Consejos-para-solucion-de-problemas?language=es) para saber cómo solucionar el problema.

# Domain Reputation Test

Quickly perform a domain reputation check to identify suspicious domains being used for abusive
behavior.
Examples include phishing, malware, spam, & disposable emails used for fake account creation, scaled bot attacks,
chargebacks, and similar types of malicious behavior.

### Trusted by thousands of companies

![](/templates/front-facing/img/customer-logos/customer-logos.svg?v=2)
![](/templates/front-facing/img/customer-logos/customer-logos.svg?v=2)
![](/templates/front-facing/img/customer-logos/customer-logos.svg?v=2)

## IPQS Domain Reputation Activity

## Domain Reputation Lookup FAQs

Domain risk scoring detects suspicious domains that may be compromised or consistently used to
facilitate fraudulent users & payments, disposable domains, or malware and phishing. Accurate domain
reputation lookups can also be provided in real-time by using our [malicious
URL scanning API endpoint](https://www.ipqualityscore.com/documentation/malicious-url-scanner-api/overview), which supports URLs or domains.

### What is a domain reputation check?

A domain reputation check measures how trustworthy or risky a domain name is based on past behavior, blacklist
status, and signs of abuse. This process identifies domains involved in phishing, malware distribution, spam
campaigns, or fake accounts — helping businesses decide whether to trust emails, signups, or web traffic from
that domain.

### Why is it important to check domain reputation?

When you check domain reputation, you protect your brand and users from potential cyber threats. A poor domain
reputation can indicate phishing activity, compromised servers, or spam origins. Regular domain reputation lookups
help reduce fraud, improve email deliverability, and maintain safe communication channels.

## Learn more about domain reputation

Accurate domain reputation lookups tap into the largest honeypot threat network online maintained by
IPQS, with over 5,000 active traps worldwide. Quickly detect new threats and the latest abuse patterns.
Identify domains frequently used for SPAM and abusive behavior with an easy domain reputation score.

# Devoteam Cloud Managed Services obtained  ISO 9001:2015 and ISO 14001:2015 certifications

##### Share

*Paris, April 18th 2024* – Devoteam is very proud to announce that our **[Cloud Managed Services](https://www.devoteam.com/expertise/cloud-managed-services/) team is now certified with two prestigious ISO certifications: ISO 14001 and ISO 9001**. These certifications are internationally recognised standards for Information Security Management Systems (ISMS).

![](data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAB4AAAAIQAQAAAABIh06PAAAAAnRSTlMAAHaTzTgAAACSSURBVHja7cExAQAAAMKg9U9tB2+gAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAeAzxHwAB9a/oWQAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==)
![](https://www.devoteam.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/ISO-9001-and-ISO-14001-1.png)

**ISO 9001** certification validates our consistent dedication to quality management systems. It signifies that our processes and procedures meet international benchmarks, ensuring consistent delivery of top-notch services to our clients. By obtaining this certification, we reaffirm our promise to provide exceptional customer satisfaction, enhance operational efficiency, and continually improve our services to meet evolving customer needs.

Equally significant is the realisation of **ISO 14001** certification, which underscores our commitment to sustainability. This certification demonstrates [our proactive approach](https://www.devoteam.com/news-and-pr/unlock-the-potential-of-sustainable-digital-transformation-with-devoteam/) to minimising our environmental footprint, reducing waste and operating in an environmentally sustainable manner. By integrating environmental management into our core business practices, we strive to not only meet regulatory requirements but also contribute positively to the global effort for environmental care.

![Quote](data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAABgAAAAYAQAAAADIDABVAAAAAnRSTlMAAHaTzTgAAAAMSURBVAjXY2CgLQAAAGAAATeQF3gAAAAASUVORK5CYII=)
![Quote](https://www.devoteam.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/citation-a-droite.svg)

Obtaining these certifications reflects our team’s relentless pursuit of excellence in every part of our operations. It underscores our dedication to meeting and exceeding the expectations of our clients.

SOURCE: https://www.iso.org/certification.html
Title: ISO - Certification
![ISO logo for print](/modules/isoorg-template/img/iso/iso-logo-print.gif)

# Certification

Certification can be a useful tool to add credibility, by demonstrating that your product or service meets the expectations of your customers. For some industries, certification is a legal or contractual requirement.

At ISO, we develop International Standards, such as [ISO 9001](/standard/62085.html "ISO 9001:2015 - Quality management systems — Requirements") and [ISO 14001](/standard/60857.html "ISO 14001:2015 - Environmental management systems "). ISO's [Committee on Conformity Assessment (CASCO)](/casco.html "CASCO") has produced a number of standards related to the certification process, which are used by certification bodies. Read more about [CASCO standards](/committee/54998.html "ISO/CASCO - Committee on conformity assessment").

**Certification** – the provision by an independent body of written assurance (a certificate) that the product, service or system in question meets specific requirements.

**Accreditation** – the formal recognition by an independent body, generally known as an accreditation body, that a certification body operates according to international standards.

## Choosing a certification body

When choosing a certification body, you should:

### How to verify an accredited certification

When seeking to verify a certification issued by an accredited certification body, you can either:

### International organizations in cooperation with ISO for accreditation

The International Accreditation Forum ([IAF](/organization/10147.html "IAF - International Accreditation Forum, Inc.")) is the world association of Conformity Assessment Accreditation Bodies and other bodies interested in conformity assessment in the fields of [management systems](/management-system-standards.html "Management system standards"), products, services, personnel, etc.

[ILAC](/organization/9452.html "ILAC - International Laboratory Accreditation Cooperation") is the international organization for accreditation bodies operating in accordance with [ISO/IEC 17011](/standard/67198.html "ISO/IEC 17011:2017 - Conformity assessment — Requirements for accreditation bodies accrediting conformity assessment bodies") and involved in the accreditation of conformity assessment bodies including calibration laboratories (using [ISO/IEC 17025](/standard/66912.html "ISO/IEC 17025:2017 - General requirements for the competence of testing and calibration laboratories")), testing laboratories (using [ISO/IEC 17025](/standard/66912.html "ISO/IEC 17025:2017 - General requirements for the competence of testing and calibration laboratories")), medical testing laboratories (using [ISO 15189](/standard/56115.html "ISO 15189:2012 - Medical laboratories — Requirements for quality and competence")), inspection bodies (using [ISO/IEC 17020](/standard/52994.html "ISO/IEC 17020:2012 - Conformity assessment — Requirements for the operation of various types of bodies performing inspection")), and proficiency testing providers (using [ISO/IEC 17043](/standard/29366.html "ISO/IEC 17043:2010 - Conformity assessment — General requirements for proficiency testing")).

## ISO does not perform certification

ISO does not perform certification or issue certificates, and it does not permit anyone to use the ISO logo in connection with certification. Certification is performed by external certification bodies, thus a company or organization cannot be certified by ISO.

## Making a complaint in relation to a claim of certification

ISO takes complaints related to the misuse of the ISO logo or false certification to ISO management system standards seriously.

Complaints should be addressed to the certification body that certified the organization. You can identify the certification body by examining the statement of certification, the certification mark used by the organization, or by requesting a copy of the certificate to the ISO standard.

If the above steps cannot be followed, please [send us an email](mailto:MSSComplaints@iso.org?subject=Complaint%20in%20relation%20to%20a%20claim%20of%20certification) with full details of the issue and how we can contact you. While ISO cannot guarantee a resolution or assume any liability, it can help facilitate dialogue between the parties involved and work towards a positive outcome.

If you have identified a false claim of an accredited certification, including misuse of the IAF logo, you may [inform the IAF Secretariat by email](mailto:secretary@iaf.nu?subject=False%20claim%20of%20an%20accredited%20certification%20or%20misuse%20of%20the%20IAF%20logo).

A response to your complaint will be sent to you within 14 days.

### [ISO Survey](/the-iso-survey.html "The ISO Survey")

Find out how many valid certificates there are in each country for the main ISO management system standards.

## Can I use the ISO logo?

The ISO logo is a registered trademark and cannot be used by anyone outside of ISO, unless they have been explicitly authorized.

If you wish to use a logo to demonstrate certification, contact the certification body that issued the certificate.

See full details about [use of the ISO logo](/iso-name-and-logo.html "ISO name and logo").

![](/files/live/sites/isoorg/files/name_and_logo/logos-dont-use.jpg/thumbnails/300)

Making lives ***easier***, ***safer*** and ***better***.

All ISO publications and materials are protected by copyright and are subject to the user’s acceptance of ISO’s conditions of copyright. Any use, including reproduction requires our written permission. All copyright requests should be addressed to [copyright@iso.org](mailto:copyright@iso.org).

With the exception of the content available through the [ISO Open data](/open-data.html "open-data") page and subject to the terms contained therein, no ISO content may be used for any machine learning and/or artificial intelligence and/or similar technologies, including but not limited to accessing or using it to (i) train data for large language or similar models, or (ii) prompt or otherwise enable artificial intelligence or similar tools to generate responses.

We are committed to ensuring that our website is accessible to everyone. If you have any questions or suggestions regarding the accessibility of this site, please [contact us](mailto:webmaster@iso.org?subject=Accessibility).

## ISO 20000 - ITSM Certificate

$100.00 $120.00

#### Duration of exam

The total duration of the exam is 1 hour (60 Minutes)

The ISO 20000 standard was formerly known as BS 15000 and is aligned with the process approach defined by the IT Infrastructure Library (ITIL)® from the United Kingdom Office of Government Commerce (OGC).

# IT Asset Management Certifications: Which One Fits You Best?

[IT Asset Management (ITAM)](https://invgate.com/guides/itam/) is the practice that can help you manage, control, and protect your IT estate, leveling up your IT offering. To run an effective practice, ITAM professionals need to engage with current industry best practices, and ITAM certifications are a massive part of this.

But since there are a lot of training courses available, we wanted to lend a helping hand and get you covered on the basics – as we already did with ITIL certifications.

Keep reading to discover everything you need to know before choosing your ITAM training!

[InvGate Asset Management 

ITAM software](https://invgate.com/asset-management)

**4.8** Gartner

★ ★ ★ ★ ★

 [See it](https://invgate.com/asset-management)

## Why do you need an IT Asset Management certification?

ITAM is a complex set of practices with a massive scope. Hardware, software, the cloud, licensing peculiarities… the list goes on. The onus is on businesses to keep themselves updated with current best practices.

In this sense, to stay relevant in the job market, [IT asset managers](/it-asset-manager-job-description) must keep on top of the latest tools, technology, and best practice guidance.

## ITAM certification training options

We all learn differently. For some, the in-class experience helps people to immerse themselves in their subject matter. Others prefer the flexibility afforded by online training. Other benefits of online training include:

* Access to a wider range of courses and programs as you're not limited by location.
* Working with a broader range of perspectives.
* Virtual courses tend to be cheaper than in-class options.
* Virtual collaboration opportunities.

On the other hand, those wanting a classroom environment may find it easier to immerse themselves in the subject. Plus, they’ll enjoy the following benefits:

* It help delegates form stronger connections to the training material and build their networks.
* It suits different learning styles.

So, it’s up to you to choose which **ITAM training option** suits you best, but we'll help you narrow down your options.

## What can you expect from IT Asset Management training?

IT Asset Management training gives you the tools and knowledge to handle every stage of the asset lifecycle with confidence. From building an accurate inventory to managing licenses, contracts, and disposals, you’ll learn how to implement efficient processes that align IT operations with business goals.  
  
Most courses also combine theory with hands-on exercises, so you can apply what you learn in real-world scenarios. Expect to gain a solid understanding of frameworks like ITIL and ISO 19770, explore automation opportunities, and strengthen your ability to make data-driven decisions that improve asset utilization and reduce costs.

## 5 best certifications for IT Asset Management

ITAM training is a crowded market, and there are many courses (and providers). To make it easier for you, we've rounded up some of the best courses by accredited training organizations.

Here’s a quick comparison of the top IT Asset Management certifications before diving into each in detail.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Certification** | **Pros** | **Cons** |
| **IAITAM** | - Offers the widest range of ITAM certifications, including CAMP, CSAM, CHAMP, CMAM, CAMSE, CITAD, CITAM, and the new CAIAM. - Globally recognized and aligned with ITAM best practices and governance standards. - Flexible online and in-person training options available. | - Completing multiple certifications can be time-intensive and costly. - Requires annual recertification to maintain credentials. |
| **Pink Elephant** | - Focused on ITAM best practices and their connection with ITSM. - Self-paced and instructor-led options available. - Simplified program structure through IT Asset Management Essentials, which covers hardware, software, mobile, and cloud assets. | - Only one ITAM-specific certification currently offered. - Less depth for those seeking advanced or specialized credentials. |
| **ITAMOrg** | - Provides globally recognized certifications aligned with ISO 19770-1 and ITIL 4. - Covers ITAM, SAM, and HAM in separate focused courses. - Developed in partnership with Van Haren Learning Solutions. | - Pricing and availability vary by authorized partner. - Advanced programs like ITAMA and SEAM are still in development. |
| **GogoTraining** | - Delivers IAITAM certifications online, including CSAM, CHAMP, and CMAM. - Offers flexible, self-paced learning at a more affordable cost (around $995–$1,295). - Ideal for professionals who prefer online study. | - Content and exams follow IAITAM’s strict certification rules. - Smaller course catalog compared to direct IAITAM or ITAMOrg programs. |
| **LISA** | - Extensive course catalog covering ITAM, SAM, HAM, ITAD, and licensing. - On-demand and live courses available 24/7. - Focused on practical, real-world applications with flexible learning options. | - Not all courses lead to formal certification (some offer certificates of completion only). - The platform is newer compared to IAITAM or ITAMOrg. |

### #1. IAITAM (International Association of Information Technology Asset Managers)

The International Association of Information Technology Asset Managers ([**IAITAM**](https://iaitam.org/)) has built a strong reputation in the ITAM industry over the past years. As an APMG-accredited organization, IAITAM is often the starting point for professionals looking to specialize in Asset Management. Its influence in shaping ITAM standards makes it a trusted choice for individuals and businesses alike.

IAITAM training courses typically range from $2,400 to $4,200, depending on the course and delivery format.

* **CAMP:** The Certified Asset Management Professional (CAMP) course is a strong overview of the IT Asset Management process while providing information on [IT Service Management (ITSM)](https://invgate.com/itsm).
* **CSAM:** The Certified Software Asset Manager (CSAM) course allows students to learn how to manage software assets within the network environment using best practices. Students will also learn how to handle challenges associated with software piracy and with software compliance necessities.
* **CHAMP:** The Certified Hardware Asset Management Professional (CHAMP) course provides advice and best practices to help professionals find ways to fit within the budget when making IT hardware purchases while also lengthening the lifespan of the current hardware.
* **CMAM:** The Certified Mobile Asset Manager (CMAM) course ensures IT professionals have the information they need to successfully manage the use of mobile devices — both BYOD and corporate-owned mobile devices — within the corporate network environment.
* **CITAD:** The Certified IT Asset Disposition (CITAD) course gives students the information they need to help the organization work through the disposal process of IT assets at the end of their useful lifespan. Practices like resale, repurposing, and recycling can help to generate financial gain for the organization.
* **CAMSE:** The Certified Asset Management Security Expert (CAMSE) course helps students learn how to incorporate security strategies that fit both IT and ITAM, enhancing the organization's overall security strategies for intrusions and attacks.
* **CITAM:** The Certified IT Asset Manager (CITAM) course is a multiple-day course, giving students an overview of ITAM and the ability to start or make improvements to an existing ITAM program within an organization. After receiving certification in this course, students can design and implement strategies for setting objectives and priorities for the ITAM program.
* **CAIAM**: The Certified Artificial Intelligence Asset Manager (CAIAM) equips participants with knowledge and skills to integrate AI into ITAM; emphasizes AI-driven insights across key process areas (KPAs).

Beyond its certification programs, IAITAM also offers several complementary initiatives to support continuous learning. Practitioners can renew credentials through the Recertification program, access materials and exams via the Education Center, or deepen their expertise with IBPL Advanced Courses based on the IAITAM Best Practice Library.

For senior professionals, the Executive Briefing provides strategic insights and real-world guidance to st

# Certified Software Asset Manager(CSAM)

The Certified Software Asset Manager program is globally designed to build expertise in software asset governance, license optimization, and IT compliance management.

Learn directly from software asset management professionals, IT governance experts, and industry leaders who are driving cost efficiency, operational control, and smarter technology investments.

### Software Asset Management Certification Exam Syllabus

### 1 SAM Introduction:+−

•SAM definition, concepts, and objectives

•Why do organizations need SAM?

•The Impact on IT asset lifecycle and business.

•The key roles in ITAM - stakeholders, functions, roles and responsibilities

•Typical business issues and challenges in CSAM workflow

•SAM best practices, framework

•The business value of SAM

•SAM from request to disposal

•Key roles and stakeholders in a SAM program

•Placement of SAM within the ITAM ecosystem

•Concept of compliance

•Risks and costs of software audits

•Organizational Challenges and Pitfalls in the SAM Process

### 2 Compliance Management Approach :+−

•Creation of a compliance event plan

•Development of compliance documentation

•Building a compliance process

•Ensuring Right to Use

•Monitoring compliance

•Effective communication in compliance management

### 3 Software License Management:+−

•Processes involved in Managing Software Licenses

•Documentation related to Software License Management

•Types of software licenses

### 4 Automation in Software Asset Management:+−

•Use of SAM tools to automate processes

•Achieving predictability in outcomes through automation

•Selection of the right tool for effective automation

### 5 Software Acquisition:+−

•Building acquisition process

•Negotiation strategies

•Non-negotiable incidents

•Cost reduction techniques

•Usage and renewals management

•Favorable terms creation

### 6 Cloud Asset Management Need and Best Practices - Managing SaaS:+−

•CIS's Definition

•NIST's Definition

•Cybersecurity best practices in cloud ITAM

### 7 Vendor Management:+−

•Acquisition, Receipt, Deployment Phase

•In-Use, Recovery, Disposal Phase

•Vendor examples

•Reports and evaluation

•Terms and Conditions

•Vendor Scorecards

### 8 IT Asset Management Program/Project Management:+−

•Processes for Managing Vendors in ITAM

•Roles and Policies related to Vendor Management

•Evaluation and Removal of Vendor Offerings

### 9 Cyber Security, Controls and ITAM:+−

•SAM in Project Management

•Management of ITAM lifecycle

•Data Retention Requirements

•Create and Maintain IT Standards

•Discovery / Reconciliation Process

•Use of Tools in ITAM

•Development of Policies

•Roles of End-Users

•On and Off-boarding process

•Vendor management practices
